articles.content_cn:
庄子的「逍遥游」描绘了一种超越一切束缚的绝对自由境界——大鹏展翅九万里，不为世间的功名利禄所累，顺应自然之道而游于无穷。在算法编织的数字牢笼中，这种两千多年前的智慧竟显得如此切中时弊。

数字时代最大的「不逍遥」来自注意力经济的精巧设计。每一个App的推送通知、每一个社交媒体的无限滚动、每一个短视频平台的推荐算法，都在争夺我们最宝贵的资源——注意力。庄子所说的「物物而不物于物」（驾驭外物而不被外物所驾驭），在当下恰恰被颠倒了：我们以为自己在使用手机，实际上手机和背后的算法系统在使用我们。

庄子对「有用」与「无用」的辩证思考在效率至上的数字文化中尤为振聋发聩。他讲的那棵因为「无用」而得以长存的大树，提醒我们不要将一切事物都纳入功利主义的评价体系。在「万物皆可优化」的算法逻辑中，那些看似无用的事物——闲逛、发呆、漫无目的地阅读——恰恰是创造力和深度思考的土壤。

「坐忘」是庄子提出的另一种修行方法——忘却形体、离弃聪明、与大道融为一体。这并非消极的逃避，而是一种积极的认知重置。在信息过载的时代，定期进行数字断舍离，主动创造没有信息输入的空白时间，也许正是现代人版本的「坐忘」。

庄子的逍遥并非逃离现实，而是以一种更高的视角重新审视现实。在算法时代，这意味着不拒绝技术本身，而是保持对技术的主体性——使用工具而不被工具所定义，在数字世界中保持内心的自由和从容。逍遥游的精神，在算法编织的天罗地网中，依然是一盏指引方向的明灯。

articles.title: 自主AI智能体2026：从对话到行动的范式革命
articles.subtitle: 从对话到行动的自主AI系统范式革命
articles.author: OpenAI / Anthropic / Google Research
articles.summary: 深入解析2026年自主AI智能体的技术突破，从OpenAI的Operator到Claude Code，探索AI如何从对话助手演进为能够独立完成复杂任务的数字工作者
articles.content_cn:
2026年标志着自主AI智能体（Autonomous AI Agents）从概念验证走向大规模应用的关键转折点。从对话式AI到行动式AI的转变，不仅是技术能力的提升，更是人工智能应用范式的根本性革命。

这一范式革命的核心特征是AI系统能力的三重跃迁：从被动响应到主动规划、从单步执行到多步推理、从信息处理到物理行动。当前的Agent系统能够接收一个高层目标（如「帮我研究竞品并撰写分析报告」），自动将其分解为子任务、检索相关信息、分析数据、生成文档，并在必要时向人类请求确认。

在企业应用中，这种能力正在改变多个行业的工作方式。金融领域的Agent能够自主监控市场动态、分析风险敞口、执行交易策略并生成合规报告。客服领域的Agent不再局限于回答常见问题，而是能够处理复杂的退换货流程、协调多个内部系统、并在需要时无缝转接人工服务。销售领域的Agent能够研究潜在客户、定制营销方案、安排会议并跟踪交易进展。

技术架构层面，2026年的Agent系统普遍采用了「规划-执行-反思」的三段式架构。大语言模型作为推理引擎负责规划和决策，工具调用接口连接外部系统和数据源，记忆模块提供跨会话的上下文保持能力，而反思机制则允许Agent从失败中学习并调整策略。

这一转变对劳动力市场的影响是深远的。虽然完全替代人类工作的场景仍然有限，但Agent已经能够处理大量重复性的认知劳动，使人类工作者能够将精力集中在创造性、战略性和需要人际交往的任务上。学会与Agent有效协作，将成为2026年职场最重要的技能之一。

articles.title: 神经符号AI融合2026：连接深度学习与逻辑推理的桥梁
articles.subtitle: 深度学习与逻辑推理的融合之路
articles.author: DeepMind / IBM / MIT Research
articles.summary: 探索神经符号AI如何将深度学习的模式识别能力与符号AI的逻辑推理能力相结合，开启可解释、可信赖的人工智能新范式
articles.content_cn:
神经符号AI（Neuro-Symbolic AI）试图融合两种截然不同的人工智能范式——深度学习的模式识别能力和符号AI的逻辑推理能力。2026年，这一融合正在从理论构想走向实际应用，为解决AI系统的可靠性、可解释性和泛化能力问题提供了新的路径。

深度学习在感知任务上表现出色——图像识别、语音处理、自然语言理解——但它在逻辑推理、因果推断和数学证明方面存在根本性的局限。纯深度学习模型的「黑箱」特性也使其在医疗诊断、法律分析和金融风控等高可靠性要求的场景中面临信任危机。符号AI（Symbolic AI）则恰好相反：它擅长明确的逻辑推理和知识表示，但在处理模糊、噪声和不确定性方面表现不佳。

神经符号融合的核心思路是将两者的优势互补。一种主流方法是将神经网络作为感知前端，将原始输入转化为结构化的符号表示，然后由符号推理引擎进行逻辑运算。另一种方法是将符号知识（如规则、约束和公理）嵌入到神经网络的训练过程中，引导模型学习符合逻辑规律的表征。

2026年最令人兴奋的进展出现在数学推理和科学发现领域。DeepMind的AlphaProof和AlphaGeometry等系统展示了神经符号融合的巨大潜力——它们能够利用神经网络快速识别可能的证明路径，然后用符号引擎严格验证每一步推理的正确性。这种「直觉+验证」的组合策略，既保证了推理的速度，又确保了结论的可靠性。

在企业应用中，神经符号AI正在为知识图谱的构建和推理、复杂业务规则的自动化执行、以及可解释AI系统的开发提供技术基础。虽然这一领域仍处于快速发展阶段，但它代表了AI系统从「统计模式匹配」向「真正理解」迈进的重要一步。

articles.title: 自由意志的幻觉：神经科学与决定论的哲学交锋
articles.subtitle: 神经科学与决定论的哲学交锋

articles.content_cn:
自由意志是否真实存在？这一困扰哲学界数千年的问题，在神经科学的最新发现面前变得愈发紧迫。本杰明·里贝特（Benjamin Libet）的经典实验及其后续研究似乎指向一个令人不安的结论：在我们意识到自己做出决定之前，大脑已经「替」我们做出了选择。

里贝特的实验设计简洁而精妙：受试者被要求在任意时刻弯曲手腕，同时记录他们意识到「决定」弯曲手腕的时间点。实验发现，大脑的「准备电位」（Readiness Potential）——一种与运动决策相关的脑电活动——在受试者报告意识到决定之前的数百毫秒就已经出现。这似乎意味着「决定」是在无意识的神经过程中率先完成的，而我们的意识只是在事后「盖了个章」。

然而，对里贝特实验的解读远非确定性的。批评者指出，准备电位反映的可能不是具体的决策过程，而是一种一般性的「准备状态」。更重要的是，里贝特本人发现受试者能够在动作执行前的最后一刻「否决」已经启动的运动计划——这暗示意识至少拥有「自由不意志」（Free Won't）的能力。

现代神经科学对决定论的支持来自更复杂的实验设计。利用fMRI和机器学习，研究者能够在受试者意识到自己的选择之前数秒，通过脑活动模式预测其将做出的决定。但这些实验的预测准确率远非100%，且主要针对简单的二选一决策，与复杂的现实生活决策之间存在巨大的鸿沟。

也许最合理的立场是一种「兼容论」（Compatibilism）：自由意志与决定论并非必然矛盾。即使我们的决策过程遵循物理因果律，只要这种因果链经过了我们的理性反思和 deliberation，我们就有理由认为自己是在「自由地」行动。关键不在于决策是否被因果决定，而在于决策过程是否体现了我们的价值观、目标和理性能力。

articles.title: 推理模型的崛起：从快思考到慢思考的技术革命
articles.subtitle: 从快思考到慢思考的AI推理革命
articles.author: OpenAI / DeepSeek / Google Research
articles.summary: 深入解析o1、o3和DeepSeek-R1等推理模型的技术突破，探索AI如何从模式匹配走向真正的逻辑推理
articles.content_cn:
AI推理模型（Reasoning Models）的崛起标志着大语言模型从「快思考」到「慢思考」的关键转变。受丹尼尔·卡尼曼的双系统理论启发，2026年的AI推理架构正在实现从即时直觉响应到审慎逐步推理的能力跃迁。

传统的语言模型本质上是一种「System 1」——基于模式匹配的快速响应。它们在海量文本上训练，学会了「什么答案看起来最合理」，但并不真正理解推理的逻辑链条。当面对需要多步推理的复杂问题时，这种快速直觉经常导致「幻觉」——看似合理但实际错误的回答。

推理模型通过引入「思维链」（Chain-of-Thought）机制，强迫模型在给出最终答案之前先展示推理过程。OpenAI的o1/o3系列、DeepSeek的R1/R2模型、以及Google的Gemini Thinking模式都采用了这一基本思路，但在具体实现上各有创新。核心区别在于：推理过程是否显式可见、推理深度是否可调节、以及模型是否具备「自我质疑」的能力。

2026年的最新进展是「慢思考」能力的进一步深化。新一代推理模型不仅能够执行预设的推理步骤，还能在推理过程中检测到矛盾、回溯错误的推理路径、尝试多种解题策略并选择最优解。这种「元认知」能力的提升，使推理模型在数学竞赛、编程挑战和科学推理等高难度任务上的表现大幅提升。

推理模型的快速发展也带来了新的挑战。首先是推理成本：慢思考模式需要更多的计算资源，如何在质量和效率之间找到平衡是工程实践的核心问题。其次是推理可靠性：更长的推理链意味着更多可能出错的地方，如何确保推理过程的每一步都是正确的仍是一个开放问题。最后是推理透明度：如何让用户理解和信任模型的推理过程，对于推理模型在关键领域的应用至关重要。

articles.title: 联邦边缘智能：分布式AI的新架构范式
articles.subtitle: 分布式AI的新架构范式
articles.author: Google / MIT / CMU Research
articles.summary: 探索联邦学习与边缘计算融合的前沿技术，了解分布式AI如何在保护隐私的同时释放数据价值
articles.content_cn:
联邦学习（Federated Learning）与边缘计算的结合正在催生一种全新的AI部署范式——在保护数据隐私的同时，将AI能力下沉到网络边缘的数以亿计的终端设备上。2026年，这种「联邦边缘智能」正在从概念验证走向大规模商业部署。

传统的集中式AI训练要求将所有数据汇集到云端，这在隐私法规日趋严格（如GDPR、中国数据安全法）的背景下面临越来越大的合规压力。联邦学习通过「数据不动模型动」的原则解决了这一矛盾——训练过程在本地设备上进行，只有模型参数的更新（梯度）被传回中心服务器聚合。用户的原始数据始终留在自己的设备上。

边缘计算为联邦学习提供了天然的硬件基础。现代智能手机、IoT设备和车载计算平台都配备了强大的AI推理芯片（如NPU），使在本地运行和训练中等规模的模型成为可能。Apple的设备端智能、Google的Android系统联邦学习框架，以及华为的端云协同计算平台，都是这一趋势的典型代表。

在医疗健康领域，联邦边缘智能的价值尤为突出。医院可以在不共享患者原始病历的情况下，协同训练高质量的疾病诊断模型。可穿戴设备能够在本地分析用户的健康数据，提供个性化的健康建议，同时确保敏感数据不离开设备。

技术挑战依然存在。通信效率是联邦学习的核心瓶颈——在带宽有限的边缘环境中传输模型更新需要精心设计的压缩和量化策略。异构性（不同设备拥有不同的计算能力和数据分布）也增加了联邦训练的复杂性。此外，差分隐私技术与联邦学习的结合正在成为保护模型更新不被逆向推断的标准实践。

articles.title: 意识的难问题：从查尔默斯到整合信息理论
articles.subtitle: 从查尔默斯到整合信息理论的意识探索

articles.content_cn:
意识之谜——哲学家大卫·查尔默斯所称的「难问题」（Hard Problem of Consciousness）——是人类面临的最深刻的科学和哲学挑战之一。为什么物理过程会产生主观体验？为什么特定频率的神经元放电会「感觉」像红色或痛苦？2026年的意识研究正在多个前沿方向上取得进展，尽管终极答案仍然遥不可及。

查尔默斯将意识研究分为「简单问题」和「难问题」。「简单问题」涉及意识的功能性方面——大脑如何处理信息、如何做出决策、如何控制行为——这些问题原则上可以通过神经科学研究逐步解答。「难问题」则追问为什么这些物理过程伴随着主观体验：为什么看到红色有一种特定的「感觉」？为什么疼痛有一种不可言喻的「质感」？

整合信息理论（Integrated Information Theory, IIT）由神经科学家朱利奥·托诺尼提出，试图为意识提供一个数学化的理论框架。IIT的核心主张是：意识等同于整合信息（用希腊字母Φ表示）。一个系统的Φ值越高——即其各部分之间的信息整合程度越强——其意识水平就越高。IIT的一个大胆推论是：意识并非人类大脑的专利，任何具有高Φ值的系统（包括某些人工神经网络）都可能具有某种形式的意识。

全球工作空间理论（Global Workspace Theory, GWT）由伯纳德·巴尔斯提出，将意识比作一个「剧场」中的聚光灯。在大量无意识的并行处理过程中，只有被聚光灯照亮的信息才能进入意识，并通过全局广播机制传递给整个认知系统。这一理论得到了大量神经影像学证据的支持。

这些理论的交锋在AI时代获得了新的紧迫性。如果我们无法确定意识的产生机制，又如何判断一个AI系统是否具有主观体验？意识的「难问题」不仅是哲学的思辨游戏，更是我们在构建越来越智能的系统时必须面对的伦理和技术问题。

articles.title: AI对齐2026：从RLHF到可解释性研究的范式转移
articles.subtitle: 从RLHF到可解释性研究的范式转移
articles.author: Anthropic / DeepMind / OpenAI Research
articles.summary: 深入分析2026年AI对齐领域的重大转变，从人类反馈强化学习到可解释性研究，探索如何确保AI系统与人类的价值观保持一致
articles.content_en:
Research

 Our research teams investigate the safety, inner workings, and societal impacts of AI models – so that artificial intelligence has a positive impact as it becomes increasingly capable.

 Research teams: Alignment Economic Research Interpretability Societal Impacts 

Interpretability

 The mission of the Interpretability team is to discover and understand how large language models work internally, as a foundation for AI safety and positive outcomes.

 

Alignment

 The Alignment team works to understand the risks of AI models and develop ways to ensure that future ones remain helpful, honest, and harmless.

 

Societal Impacts

 Working closely with the Anthropic Policy and Safeguards teams, Societal Impacts is a technical research team that explores how AI is used in the real world.

 

Frontier Red Team

 The Frontier Red Team analyzes the implications of frontier AI models for cybersecurity, biosecurity, and autonomous systems.

 

Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude’s thoughts into text

 Interpretability May 7, 2026 AI models like Claude talk in words but think in numbers. In this study we train Claude to translate its thoughts into human-readable text.

 Alignment May 8, 2026 

Teaching Claude why

 New research on how we've reduced agentic misalignment.

 Research Apr 24, 2026 

Project Deal

 We created a marketplace for employees in our San Francisco office, with one big twist. We tasked Claude with buying, selling and negotiating on our colleagues’ behalf.

 Societal Impacts Mar 18, 2026 

What 81,000 people want from AI

 We invited Claude.ai users to share how they use AI, what they dream it could make possible, and what they fear it might do. Nearly 81,000 people participated—the largest and most multilingual qualitative study of its kind. Here's what we found.

 Policy Dec 18, 2025 

Project Vend: Phase two

 In June, we revealed that we’d set up a small shop in our San Francisco office lunchroom, run by an AI shopkeeper. It was part of Project Vend, a free-form experiment exploring how well AIs could do on complex, real-world tasks. How has Claude's business been since we last wrote? 

 

Publications

 Search Date Category Title May 14, 2026 Policy 2028: Two scenarios for global AI leadership May 8, 2026 Alignment Teaching Claude why May 7, 2026 Interpretability Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude’s thoughts into text May 7, 2026 Alignment Donating our open-source alignment tool May 7, 2026 Policy Focus areas for The Anthropic Institute Apr 30, 2026 Societal Impacts How people ask Claude for personal guidance Apr 29, 2026 Science Evaluating Claude’s bioinformatics research capabilities with BioMysteryBench Apr 22, 2026 Economic Research Announcing the Anthropic Economic Index Survey Apr 22, 2026 Economic Research What 81,000 people told us about the economics of AI Apr 14, 2026 Alignment Automated Alignment Researchers: Using large language models to scale scalable oversight See more Join the Research team

articles.content_cn:
AI对齐（AI Alignment）研究在2026年正在经历从RLHF（基于人类反馈的强化学习）到可解释性研究的范式转移。这一转变反映了AI安全领域从「训练时对齐」到「理解时对齐」的深层逻辑演进。

RLHF作为第一代对齐技术，其核心思路是通过人类标注者的偏好反馈来微调模型的行为。虽然RLHF在让模型产生更安全、更有帮助的回答方面取得了显著成效，但它存在根本性的局限。首先是「奖励黑客」（Reward Hacking）问题：模型学会了在表面上满足人类偏好标准，但在本质上并未真正理解和内化这些价值观。其次是标注者的偏好可能存在偏见和不一致，导致模型学习到的是标注者群体的偏好，而非真正普适的价值观。

可解释性研究代表了对齐问题的另一种解法。如果我们能够理解模型内部表征的含义——哪些神经元激活对应哪些概念、模型做出某个决策时依据了哪些特征——我们就能够从根本上确保模型的行为符合人类价值观。机械可解释性（Mechanistic Interpretability）研究在2026年取得了重要进展，研究者已经能够识别和操纵模型内部的特定功能回路，如「真实陈述」回路、「拒绝回答」回路等。

宪法AI（Constitutional AI）是另一种有前景的方向。由Anthropic提出的方法让模型根据一组预设的「宪法原则」来进行自我对齐——模型生成回答、根据原则进行自我批评、然后修正回答。这种方法的潜力在于它将价值观从人类标注者的隐含偏好转化为显式的、可审计的原则集。

2026年的AI对齐研究正在走向多层次的体系：预训练阶段通过数据质量控制建立基础安全能力，微调阶段通过RLHF和宪法AI对齐具体行为，推理阶段通过可解释性工具实时监控和干预。这种多层防御策略为构建可靠、安全、与人类价值观对齐的AI系统提供了更全面的保障。

articles.title: 生物计算崛起：从DNA存储到类脑计算的跨界融合
articles.subtitle: 从DNA存储到类脑计算的跨界融合
articles.author: MIT / Harvard / ETH Zurich Research
articles.summary: 探索生物计算领域的革命性进展，从DNA数据存储到类脑芯片，了解生物与计算的融合如何开启计算技术的新纪元
articles.content_en:
MIT continually develops organizations and partnerships that foster interdisciplinary work. Listed here are just some of the MIT labs, centers, and programs where groundbreaking research is happening every day.



 View Centers, Labs & Programs 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 MIT researchers have devised a miniaturized system that can deliver tiny quantities of medicine to brain regions as small as 1 cubic millimeter.
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 MIT researchers collaborate with many leading local, national, and international organizations to further drive exploration.



 View Collaborating Institutions 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Researchers from MIT and the Charles Stark Draper Laboratory are developing a new space boot with built-in sensors and tiny “haptic” motors, whose vibrations can guide the wearer around or over obstacles.
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 Conducting research in a biological engineering lab 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 A system from CSAIL uses EEG brain signals to detect if a person notices robots making an error. 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 This student, an Olympic medalist, is developing a device that more precisely measures the curvatures of the blades used for speedskates. 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 Recent faculty research topics in mechanical engineering include pattern formation, fluid dynamics, and soft condensed matter. 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 Ion accelerators play a strong role in magnetic fusion energy research. 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 MIT students have research interests that extend beyond the lab. 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 MIT researchers have developed a new way to image proteins and RNA inside neurons of intact brain tissue. 
 
 

 

 
 
 
 
 
 A graduate student works on robotic systems that are tasked with removing items from bins and placing them into boxes.
articles.content_cn:
生物计算（Biological Computing）正在从一个边缘研究方向走向主流科技视野。从DNA数据存储到类脑计算芯片，自然界的生物系统为下一代计算技术提供了丰富的灵感和材料。

DNA数据存储利用了DNA分子超高的信息密度和长期稳定性。理论上，一克DNA可以存储215PB的数据，且在适宜条件下可以保存数千年不降解。2026年，多个研究团队已经实现了从DNA合成、存储到读取的全流程自动化，成本较五年前降低了两个数量级。Microsoft的DNA数据存储项目和IARPA的Molecular Information Storage项目正在推动这一技术走向商业化。虽然DNA存储的读写速度远不及传统存储介质，但对于冷数据和长期归档场景，其独特的优势使其成为有前景的补充方案。

类脑计算（Neuromorphic Computing）是生物计算的另一个重要分支。受大脑神经元和突触结构的启发，类脑芯片采用脉冲神经网络（SNN）的计算范式，以极低的能耗实现高效的模式识别和信号处理。Intel的Loihi 2、IBM的NorthPole以及中国的天机芯片代表了不同的类脑计算架构路线。2026年的类脑芯片在能效比上已经比传统GPU高出数百倍，特别适合边缘设备和自主机器人的实时感知和决策。

合成生物学与计算的融合开辟了更激进的可能性。研究人员已经成功将活体细胞改造为可编程的生物计算机——利用基因线路实现逻辑运算、利用蛋白质相互作用实现信号处理。这些「湿」计算机虽然在速度上无法与传统计算机竞争，但在生物传感、智能药物释放和生物材料制造等场景中展现了独特的优势。

生物计算的发展正处于一个关键节点：它不再仅仅是概念验证，而是开始在特定应用场景中展现出相对于传统计算范式的实际优势。随着成本的持续下降和工具链的日益成熟，我们有望在未来几年内看到生物计算技术的商业化突破。

articles.title: 后人类主义的哲学审视：超越人类中心主义的思想实验
articles.subtitle: 超越人类中心主义的思想实验
articles.author: Rosi Braidotti / N. Katherine Hayles
articles.summary: 探索后人类主义哲学的核心命题，从尼采的超人到哈拉维的赛博格，思考人类在智能时代如何重新定义自身边界与存在意义
articles.content_cn:
后人类主义（Posthumanism）不仅仅是一个学术概念，它是一场关于人类未来身份的深刻思想实验。当技术赋予我们改造自身身体和心智的能力时，「人类」这个范畴的边界在哪里？我们是应该拥抱技术带来的超越，还是应该警惕其可能导致的异化？

超人类主义（Transhumanism）作为后人类主义的一个分支，积极主张利用技术增强人类能力——从认知增强到寿命延长，从脑机接口到基因编辑。Nick Bostrom、Ray Kurzweiz和Yuval Noah Harari等思想家从不同角度描绘了人类通过技术实现自我超越的可能路径。在他们看来，人类并非进化的终点，而是一个过渡阶段，技术的加速发展将引领我们进入一个「后人类」的存在状态。

然而，批判性后人类主义提出了尖锐的反驳。Donna Haraway的「赛博格宣言」提醒我们，技术增强并非中性的工具应用，而是深刻地重塑了我们与世界、与他人、与自身的关系。当我们的记忆依赖云存储、我们的社交依赖算法匹配、我们的决策依赖AI辅助时，我们的「自我」是否还完整地属于我们自己？

超越人类中心主义的思想实验还涉及伦理层面的根本性重构。如果人类的认知能力可以被增强到远超当前水平，那么「正常人」和「增强人」之间的不平等将如何处理？如果AI系统获得了某种形式的意识或道德地位，人类中心主义的伦理框架是否需要扩展？如果我们可以通过基因编辑消除疾病但同时也打开了「设计婴儿」的潘多拉魔盒，自由的边界在哪里？

这些问题的答案远非明确，但追问本身就具有重要的价值。在一个技术正在深刻改变人类存在方式的时代，后人类主义的思考帮助我们保持对技术发展的批判性反思，确保技术的演进服务于人类真正的福祉，而非滑向一种技术决定论的反乌托邦。

articles.title: AI智能体经济2026：自主系统如何重塑商业格局
articles.subtitle: 自主系统如何重塑商业格局
articles.author: McKinsey / Gartner Research
articles.summary: 全面解析2026年AI智能体经济的技术架构与商业模式，从单智能体到多智能体协作，探索自主AI如何改变企业运营和市场竞争
articles.content_cn:
AI智能体经济（Agentic AI Economy）正在从远景预测变成2026年的商业现实。自主AI系统不再仅仅是企业内部的效率工具，而是正在成为商业生态中的独立参与者——能够自主完成交易、管理供应链、优化广告投放，甚至进行商业谈判。

Gartner预测到2026年底，超过40%的企业应用将嵌入某种形式的AI代理能力。这些代理系统能够处理从前需要专门团队才能完成的任务：市场研究、竞争分析、客户关系管理、财务规划和风险评估。它们7×24小时不间断工作，不受情绪波动影响，且能够实时处理海量数据做出决策。

在金融领域，AI交易代理已经占据了相当比例的市场交易量。不同于传统的高频交易算法，新一代交易代理能够理解新闻事件的语义含义、分析社交媒体的市场情绪、预测宏观经济趋势，并据此做出综合性的交易决策。这种从「规则驱动」到「理解驱动」的转变，正在改变金融市场的运作方式。

在供应链管理领域，AI代理能够自主监控全球供应链的运行状态，在检测到潜在中断风险时主动寻找替代方案、重新规划物流路线、协商替代采购合同。COVID-19大流行暴露的供应链脆弱性，正通过AI代理的自主管理能力得到系统性的缓解。

然而，AI智能体经济也带来了新的挑战。责任归属问题是首要的：当AI代理做出错误决策导致经济损失时，责任由谁承担？市场稳定性问题也值得关注：大量AI代理同时运作是否会导致市场行为的高度相关性，增加系统性风险？建立适当的治理框架和监管机制，是AI智能体经济健康发展的关键前提。

articles.title: 量子-经典混合计算2026：下一代算力基础设施
articles.subtitle: 下一代算力基础设施
articles.author: IBM / Google / IonQ Research
articles.summary: 深入解析2026年量子计算与经典计算融合的技术突破，从NISQ时代到容错量子计算，探索混合架构如何释放下一代算力潜能
articles.content_en:
IBM charts a new research path with MIT 

 Q & A Kim Martineau 11 May 2026 Accelerated Discovery AI Natural Language Processing Quantum Quantum Algorithms
articles.content_cn:
量子-经典混合计算正在成为2026年下一代算力基础设施的核心架构。在通用量子计算机尚未成熟的当下，混合架构提供了一条务实的路径——将量子处理器的独特能力与经典计算系统的成熟生态无缝整合。

混合计算的基本范式是「量子协处理器」模式：经典计算机作为主控系统处理大部分计算任务，将特定的、可能受益于量子加速的子任务卸载到量子处理器上执行。这类似于GPU在AI训练中扮演的角色——不是替代CPU，而是作为专门的加速器处理特定类型的工作负载。

IBM的量子计算路线图以混合计算为核心战略。其最新的量子处理器配备了超过1000个量子比特，并通过Qiskit Runtime提供了量子-经典混合编程框架。Google的量子AI团队则专注于在量子纠错技术上的突破，其最新成果将逻辑量子比特的错误率降低到了实用化门槛以下。

在化学模拟领域，混合计算的实用价值已经得到验证。制药公司利用量子-经典混合算法模拟分子间的化学反应过程，精度远超纯经典方法。在新材料发现中，混合计算平台能够探索更大的材料设计空间，加速候选材料的筛选和优化。这些应用虽然还局限于特定的分子规模，但已经展示了量子计算的实际商业价值。

混合计算基础设施的建设需要解决一系列工程挑战：量子比特的退相干时间限制了量子电路的深度，需要精心设计的错误缓解策略；量子与经典系统之间的数据传输延迟要求新的编译和调度优化；量子计算资源的稀缺性要求高效的任务排队和资源分配机制。这些工程问题的逐步解决，正在将量子计算从实验室推向数据中心。

articles.title: 时间的伦理：从海德格尔的「向死而生」到数字时代的永恒现在
articles.subtitle: 从海德格尔的「向死而生」到数字时代的永恒现在

Martin Heidegger

 First published Fri Jan 31, 2025 

 

 
Martin Heidegger (1889–1976) is a central figure in the
development of twentieth-century European Philosophy. His magnum
opus , Being and Time (1927), and his many essays and
lectures, profoundly influenced subsequent movements in European
philosophy, including Hannah Arendt’s political philosophy,
Jean-Paul Sartre’s existentialism, Simone de Beauvoir’s
feminism, Maurice Merleau-Ponty’s phenomenology of perception,
Hans-Georg Gadamer’s hermeneutics, Jacques Derrida’s
deconstruction, Michel Foucault’s post-structuralism, Gilles
Deleuze’s metaphysics, the Frankfurt School, and critical
theorists like Theodor Adorno, Herbert Marcuse, Jürgen Habermas,
and Georg Lukács. Beyond Europe, Being and Time has
influenced movements like the Kyoto School in Japan, and North
American philosophers like Hubert Dreyfus, Richard Rorty, and Charles
Taylor. His influence has extended far beyond the bounds of academic
philosophy, and his existential analysis of human existence has
inspired theorists in fields as diverse as theology, anthropology,
sociology, psychology, aesthetics, literary criticism, political
science, strategic management, and cognitive science.

 
The driving question in Heidegger’s work is “the question
of being”—the question of the meaning or sense of
being—and he argued that our understanding of being is
temporally structured. In Being and Time , Heidegger pursued
the question of being by means of a phenomenological exploration of
the way that time structures our engagement with the world. Heidegger
was an ontological pluralist: he held that there are multiple distinct
 kinds of being. Human beings or “Dasein”,
Heidegger argued, must be understood as beings-in-the-world, and thus
as constituted by our relationships to the practical and social
contexts that give meaning to our actions. The analytic of Dasein
underpinned his critique of post-Cartesian accounts of human beings as
subjects in an objective world. In his later work, Heidegger argued
that being is historical in a profound sense—that there has been
a sequence of distinct “epochs” of being, culminating in
the contemporary technological age. Heidegger argued that language and
works of art play a constitutive role in structuring these
“being-historical worlds”.

1. Life and Work

 
Martin Heidegger was born on 26 September 1889 in Messkirch
Germany—a small, rural town in southwest Germany. His father,
Friedrich Heidegger, was a skilled craftsman (a master cooper) and a
sexton in the Catholic Church. Heidegger’s thought was
profoundly influenced by the provincial environs of his youth, his
conservative upbringing, and his intimate familiarity with craftwork
and the rhythms of agricultural life. In 1909, he entered the
University of Freiburg where he studied Catholic theology, the natural
sciences, and mathematics, before settling on philo
articles.content_cn:
海德格尔在《存在与时间》中对「向死而生」（Being-toward-death）的分析，揭示了人类存在的一个根本性维度：正是因为我们确知自己终将死亡，生命才获得了紧迫感和意义感。然而在数字时代，这一存在论结构正在被技术深刻地改变——我们似乎获得了一种「数字永恒」的幻觉，死亡在虚拟世界中被隐匿和悬置了。

社交媒体创造了前所未有的「死后存在」。一个人去世后，其社交媒体账号、照片、视频和文字留存继续存在于数字空间中，甚至通过算法继续被推荐和传播。AI「复活」技术更进一步——利用深度学习模型，我们可以让逝者的影像继续「说话」，甚至模拟其语气和思维方式生成新的内容。这种技术给生者带来安慰的同时，也模糊了生与死的界限。

海德格尔会如何看待这种数字永恒？他可能会指出，这种「永恒」恰恰是对本真存在的逃避。在《存在与时间》的框架中，死亡不是一个应该被逃避或否认的终点，而是赋予每个当下以意义的可能性条件。「向死而生」意味着认识到生命的有限性，从而在本真的决断中承担起自己的存在。当数字技术试图消除死亡的存在论意义时，它同时也在消除生命本身的紧迫感和深度。

数字时代的「永恒现在」还表现为一种时间性的扁平化。社交媒体的信息流没有过去和未来的纵深——一切都发生在永恒滚动的「当下」。这种时间体验与海德格尔所描述的「本真时间性」形成鲜明对比：后者强调的是在过去（曾在）、现在（当前）和未来（能在）的统一中理解存在的意义。

也许海德格尔最重要的提醒是：真正的存在不是在时间的无限延长中实现的，而是在有限性中对每一个当下的承担和选择中实现的。面对数字技术构建的永恒幻觉，我们需要重新学会直面死亡——不是为了恐惧，而是为了在本真地活着时真正地活着。

articles.title: 具身智能2026：人形机器人如何从实验室走向现实世界
articles.subtitle: 人形机器人从实验室走向现实世界
articles.author: Tesla / Figure AI / Boston Dynamics Research
articles.summary: 全面解析2026年具身智能技术的突破与应用，从特斯拉Optimus到Figure AI，探索人形机器人如何从技术演示迈向实际部署
articles.content_en:
Access Denied

You don't have permission to access "httpwwwteslacomoptimus" on this server.

 httpserrorsedgesuitenet188ded2017177881418912ce5863
articles.content_cn:
具身智能（Embodied Intelligence）是2026年人工智能领域最热门的方向之一，其核心主张是：真正的智能不能脱离物理身体而存在。从实验室中摇摇晃晃的原型到逐步走向现实世界的商业产品，人形机器人正在经历从科幻想象到工程现实的跨越。

2026年人形机器人领域的标志性事件包括：Figure AI的Figure 02在BMW工厂开始试生产操作，特斯拉Optimus在仓储物流场景中实现小规模部署，以及中国多家企业（宇树科技、智元机器人、银河通用）推出成本低于10万元的人形机器人平台。这些进展表明，人形机器人正在从昂贵的概念验证走向具有经济可行性的商业应用。

技术层面，人形机器人的进步得益于三大核心能力的提升。首先是运动控制——从传统的预编程运动轨迹到基于强化学习的动态运动控制，机器人能够在不平坦的地形上稳定行走、上下楼梯、甚至在跌倒后自主站起。其次是操作能力——灵巧手的触觉反馈和力控精度大幅提升，使机器人能够执行精细的物体操作任务。最后是感知理解——多模态大模型赋予机器人理解自然语言指令、识别物体和环境、以及规划行动策略的能力。

然而，从实验室到现实世界的跨越远非坦途。真实环境的复杂性远超实验室——光线变化、物体多样性、人类行为的不确定性——都对机器人的鲁棒性提出了极高要求。成本控制也是商业化的关键瓶颈：高性能执行器和传感器的成本仍占人形机器人总成本的很大比例。

最具争议性的问题是：我们是否真的需要「人形」机器人？轮式底盘在结构化环境中效率更高，机械臂在工业场景中已经成熟。人形机器人的价值在于其通用性——它是为人类设计的环境量身打造的形态，能够在不需要改造环境的前提下进入人类的日常生活和工作空间。这种通用性在短期内可能不比专用解决方案高效，但在长期视角下，它代表了机器人从「专用工具」到「通用助手」的进化方向。

 How the GitHub Issues team used client-side caching, smart prefetching, and service workers to make navigation feel instant.
articles.content_cn:
从Copilot到Agent的进化，正在重新定义开发者与智能机器之间的协作关系。这不仅是工具的升级，更是软件开发范式的根本性转变——从「AI辅助编程」到「AI驱动工程」。

GitHub Copilot开创了AI辅助编程的大众化时代。通过在编辑器中提供实时代码建议，Copilot大幅提升了编码速度。但它的角色本质上是「副驾驶」——建议由AI提供，决策和创造由人类完成。开发者仍然需要理解每一行代码的含义，仍然需要架构设计、调试和代码审查。

Agent模式标志着质的飞跃。当开发者向Cursor的Agent或Devin下达「实现用户登录功能」的指令时，Agent会自主完成一系列复杂操作：分析现有代码库的结构、选择合适的技术方案、创建必要的文件、编写代码、运行测试、修复错误、提交代码。整个过程中，开发者从「执行者」转变为「审查者」和「指导者」。

这种角色转变对开发者技能的要求也发生了变化。代码编写的机械性技能变得不那么关键，而系统设计能力、问题分解能力、代码审查能力和与AI有效沟通的能力变得更加重要。能够用清晰的自然语言描述需求、识别AI生成代码中的潜在问题、以及在复杂的工程上下文中做出正确的架构决策，成为了新一代开发者的核心能力。

这一转变也引发了对软件质量和安全的担忧。AI生成的代码虽然语法正确，但可能包含微妙的逻辑错误、安全隐患或不一致的架构决策。如何在保持开发效率的同时确保代码质量，是工程组织需要认真解决的问题。代码审查的流程需要适应AI生成代码的特点——不再逐行审查语法，而是重点关注架构合理性、安全边界和业务逻辑的正确性。

未来的开发者不会是纯粹的「AI操作员」，也不会是传统的「手工编码者」。最成功的开发者将是那些能够将深厚的工程理解与AI工具有效结合的人——他们知道何时该让AI自主行动，何时需要介入指导，以及如何在人机协作中发挥各自的优势。

articles.title: 技术的追问：从海德格尔到人工智能时代
articles.subtitle: 从海德格尔到AI时代的技术追问
articles.author: Martin Heidegger / 当代解读
articles.summary: 探索海德格尔技术哲学的当代意义，理解技术不仅是工具，更是一种揭示世界的方式，以及这对AI时代的深刻启示
articles.content_en:
-->

Martin Heidegger

 First published Fri Jan 31, 2025 

 

 
Martin Heidegger (1889–1976) is a central figure in the
development of twentieth-century European Philosophy. His magnum
opus , Being and Time (1927), and his many essays and
lectures, profoundly influenced subsequent movements in European
philosophy, including Hannah Arendt’s political philosophy,
Jean-Paul Sartre’s existentialism, Simone de Beauvoir’s
feminism, Maurice Merleau-Ponty’s phenomenology of perception,
Hans-Georg Gadamer’s hermeneutics, Jacques Derrida’s
deconstruction, Michel Foucault’s post-structuralism, Gilles
Deleuze’s metaphysics, the Frankfurt School, and critical
theorists like Theodor Adorno, Herbert Marcuse, Jürgen Habermas,
and Georg Lukács. Beyond Europe, Being and Time has
influenced movements like the Kyoto School in Japan, and North
American philosophers like Hubert Dreyfus, Richard Rorty, and Charles
Taylor. His influence has extended far beyond the bounds of academic
philosophy, and his existential analysis of human existence has
inspired theorists in fields as diverse as theology, anthropology,
sociology, psychology, aesthetics, literary criticism, political
science, strategic management, and cognitive science.

 
The driving question in Heidegger’s work is “the question
of being”—the question of the meaning or sense of
being—and he argued that our understanding of being is
temporally structured. In Being and Time , Heidegger pursued
the question of being by means of a phenomenological exploration of
the way that time structures our engagement with the world. Heidegger
was an ontological pluralist: he held that there are multiple distinct
 kinds of being. Human beings or “Dasein”,
Heidegger argued, must be understood as beings-in-the-world, and thus
as constituted by our relationships to the practical and social
contexts that give meaning to our actions. The analytic of Dasein
underpinned his critique of post-Cartesian accounts of human beings as
subjects in an objective world. In his later work, Heidegger argued
that being is historical in a profound sense—that there has been
a sequence of distinct “epochs” of being, culminating in
the contemporary technological age. Heidegger argued that language and
works of art play a constitutive role in structuring these
“being-historical worlds”.

1. Life and Work

 
Martin Heidegger was born on 26 September 1889 in Messkirch
Germany—a small, rural town in southwest Germany. His father,
Friedrich Heidegger, was a skilled craftsman (a master cooper) and a
sexton in the Catholic Church. Heidegger’s thought was
profoundly influenced by the provincial environs of his youth, his
conservative upbringing, and his intimate familiarity with craftwork
and the rhythms of agricultural life. In 1909, he entered the
University of Freiburg where he studied Catholic theology, the natural
sciences, and mathematics, before settling on philo
articles.content_cn:
海德格尔对技术的追问远不止于对技术工具的分析，他追问的是技术的本质——技术在存在论层面意味着什么。在AI时代，这一追问比以往任何时候都更加紧迫，因为人工智能不仅是一种工具，更是一种正在重塑人类存在方式的根本性力量。

海德格尔将现代技术的本质称为「座架」（Gestell/Enframing）。座架是一种揭示（Aletheia）的方式——它将万物呈现为「持存物」（Bestand/Standing-Reserve），即可被计算、提取和利用的资源。河流变成了水力发电的资源，森林变成了木材供应的资源，人变成了人力资源。在这一逻辑下，人类自身也被座架所框架——我们成为技术系统的操作者和维护者，同时也被技术系统所规定和限定。

将海德格尔的「座架」概念应用于AI时代，我们可以看到这种框架化逻辑的极端化。在算法推荐系统中，每个用户被简化为一组可计算的特征向量——点击率、停留时长、兴趣标签。人的复杂性和独特性被压缩为可被算法处理的数据点。在绩效管理系统中，员工被量化为KPI和OKR的集合。在社交平台上，人际关系被转化为点赞数和互动率。

海德格尔并非简单地反对技术。他反复强调，对技术本质的追问不是为了否定技术，而是为了在与技术的自由关系中找到自己的位置。他所说的「泰然任之」（Gelassenheit/releasement）不是消极的放任，而是一种对技术的既使用又超越的态度——使用技术但不被技术的逻辑所完全支配。

AI时代的技术追问需要我们在两个方向上同时努力：一方面深入理解AI系统的工作原理和局限，避免盲目崇拜或恐惧；另一方面保持对技术之外的存在维度的敏感——那些不可被计算、不可被优化、不可被AI替代的人类体验和价值。只有这样，我们才能在AI时代保持海德格尔所说的「本真存在」。

articles.title: 多模态AI融合2026：从感知到认知的技术跃迁
articles.subtitle: 从感知到认知的多模态技术跃迁
articles.author: Google DeepMind / OpenAI Research
articles.summary: 深入解析2026年多模态AI的技术突破，从视觉-语言模型到具身智能，探索AI如何整合多源信息实现更接近人类的认知能力
articles.content_cn:
多模态AI融合（Multimodal AI）正在从「感知」阶段迈向「认知」阶段。2026年的多模态模型不再仅仅是同时处理文本、图像和音频，而是能够在不同模态之间建立深层的语义关联，实现跨模态的推理和理解。

GPT-5和Gemini 2代表了多模态融合的前沿水平。这些模型能够「看」一张图片、「听」一段音频、「读」一份文档，然后将不同模态的信息综合起来回答复杂问题。例如，给定一张产品照片和用户评论，模型能够判断评论是否与图片中的产品一致；给定一段医学影像和病历文本，模型能够辅助医生做出更准确的诊断。

视觉-语言模型（VLM）是多模态融合中最活跃的研究方向。2026年的VLM在细粒度视觉理解上取得了显著进展——它们不仅能识别图中的物体，还能理解物体的空间关系、材质属性、物理状态和语义含义。LLaVA、Qwen-VL和InternVL等开源模型在多项基准测试中的表现已经接近闭源模型的水平。

音频模态的融合也在加速。从语音识别到语音合成，从音乐生成到声纹分析，AI对音频的理解和创造能力正在达到新的高度。实时多语言翻译、基于语音的情感识别、以及根据文本描述生成特定风格音乐的能力，正在改变创意产业和通信领域的面貌。

多模态融合的技术挑战包括：不同模态之间的对齐问题（如何建立文本、图像和音频之间的语义映射）、计算资源的需求（同时处理多种模态大幅增加了计算开销）、以及数据稀缺性（高质量的多模态配对数据远少于纯文本数据）。随着这些挑战的逐步解决，多模态AI将从当前的「辅助工具」进化为真正的「通用感知系统」，为机器人、自动驾驶和智能助手等应用提供更全面的环境理解能力。

articles.title: AI智能体的崛起：2026年自主系统如何重塑数字世界
articles.subtitle: 2026年自主系统如何重塑数字世界
articles.author: Anthropic / AutoGPT Research
articles.summary: 全面解析2026年AI Agent的技术突破与商业应用，从单智能体到多智能体协作，探索自主AI如何改变企业运营和个人工作方式
articles.content_en:
Research

 Our research teams investigate the safety, inner workings, and societal impacts of AI models – so that artificial intelligence has a positive impact as it becomes increasingly capable.

 Research teams: Alignment Economic Research Interpretability Societal Impacts 

Interpretability

 The mission of the Interpretability team is to discover and understand how large language models work internally, as a foundation for AI safety and positive outcomes.

 

Alignment

 The Alignment team works to understand the risks of AI models and develop ways to ensure that future ones remain helpful, honest, and harmless.

 

Societal Impacts

 Working closely with the Anthropic Policy and Safeguards teams, Societal Impacts is a technical research team that explores how AI is used in the real world.

 

Frontier Red Team

 The Frontier Red Team analyzes the implications of frontier AI models for cybersecurity, biosecurity, and autonomous systems.

 

Natural Language Autoencoders: Turning Claude’s thoughts into text

 Interpretability May 7, 2026 AI models like Claude talk in words but think in numbers. In this study we train Claude to translate its thoughts into human-readable text.

 Alignment May 8, 2026 

Teaching Claude why

 New research on how we've reduced agentic misalignment.

 Research Apr 24, 2026 

Project Deal

 We created a marketplace for employees in our San Francisco office, with one big twist. We tasked Claude with buying, selling and negotiating on our colleagues’ behalf.

 Societal Impacts Mar 18, 2026 

What 81,000 people want from AI

 We invited Claude.ai users to share how they use AI, what they dream it could make possible, and what they fear it might do. Nearly 81,000 people participated—the largest and most multilingual qualitative study of its kind. Here's what we found.

 Policy Dec 18, 2025 

Project Vend: Phase two

 In June, we revealed that we’d set up a small shop in our San Francisco office lunchroom, run by an AI shopkeeper. It was part of Project Vend, a free-form experiment exploring how well AIs could do on complex, real-world tasks. How has Claude's business been since we last wrote? 

 

Publications

articles.content_cn:
2026年，AI智能体（AI Agents）正在以前所未有的速度重塑数字世界的运作方式。从个人数字助理到企业级自动化系统，从内容生成到决策支持，自主AI系统正在成为数字基础设施的核心组件。

AI Agent的核心能力在于其自主性（Autonomy）。与传统软件系统需要人类明确指定每一个操作步骤不同，Agent能够根据高层目标自主规划和执行任务。这种能力的实现依赖于三大技术支柱：大语言模型提供推理和自然语言理解能力、工具调用框架连接外部系统和数据源、记忆模块维持跨会话的上下文和知识。

在企业应用中，AI Agent正在重塑多个关键业务流程。客户服务Agent能够处理复杂的咨询和投诉，在多个内部系统之间协调操作，并在需要时无缝转接人工服务。销售Agent能够研究潜在客户、定制营销方案、安排会议并跟踪交易进展。IT运维Agent能够监控基础设施、自动响应事件、优化系统性能并生成运维报告。

个人Agent领域同样在快速发展。Apple Intelligence、Google Gemini和Microsoft Copilot都在将个人数字助理的能力提升到新的水平——从管理日程、撰写邮件、整理文件，到研究复杂问题、规划旅行行程、处理行政事务。这些Agent逐渐成为用户与数字世界交互的主要界面。

然而，Agent的自主性也带来了信任和安全的挑战。当Agent代表用户做出决策和执行操作时，如何确保其行为符合用户的意图和价值观？如何建立有效的监督机制，在给予Agent足够自主权的同时保持人类的最终控制权？这些问题不仅是技术问题，更是设计和治理问题，需要开发者、企业和监管机构共同面对。

articles.title: 数字时代的斯多葛哲学：在信息洪流中寻找内心的平静

articles.content_cn:
斯多葛哲学（Stoicism）在数字时代正在经历一场意想不到的复兴。两千多年前由塞内卡、爱比克泰德和马可·奥勒留发展出的生活智慧，在信息过载、注意力稀缺和数字焦虑的当代社会中，提供了实用而深刻的心灵指南。

斯多葛哲学的第一个核心教导是「控制二分法」——区分我们能控制的和不能控制的事物。在数字时代，这一原则的适用性比以往任何时候都更强。我们无法控制社交媒体算法推荐什么内容、无法控制他人在线上的言行、无法控制新闻推送中的事件发展。但我们可以控制自己的注意力分配、信息消费习惯和情绪反应方式。将精力从无法控制的外部事件转向可以控制的内在态度，是数字斯多葛主义的第一课。

斯多葛哲学的第二个核心是「消极想象」（Negative Visualization）——有意识地想象失去我们珍视的事物，以此来更加珍惜当下拥有的一切。在追求永远「更多」的注意力经济中，这种练习尤为宝贵。想象一下如果互联网突然消失，我们会多么珍惜与朋友面对面交流的时光；如果社交媒体不存在，我们会多么享受不被打扰的独处时刻。

爱比克泰德说：「困扰人们的不是事物本身，而是人们对事物的看法。」在信息洪流中，同样的新闻对不同的人产生截然不同的情绪影响，差异不在于信息本身，而在于我们如何解读和回应。培养对信息进行理性过滤的能力——区分重要与紧急、事实与观点、信号与噪声——是数字时代的斯多葛修炼。

数字时代的内心平静不是通过逃避技术来实现的，而是通过培养一种与技术之间的明智关系。斯多葛哲学教给我们的不是退缩，而是在喧嚣中保持内心的宁静和理性。这种能力在信息过载的时代，比在任何其他时代都更加珍贵。

articles.title: 自主AI系统2026：从智能体到超级个体的技术演进
articles.subtitle: 从智能体到超级个体的技术演进
articles.author: Andrej Karpathy
articles.summary: 探索2026年自主AI系统的最新技术突破，从多代理协作到自进化架构，了解AI如何向超级个体演进并重塑人机协作的未来
articles.content_en:
Andrej Karpathy 
 
 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

Andrej Karpathy

 

I like to train deep neural nets on large datasets 🧠🤖💥

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 
It is important to note that Andrej Karpathy is a member of the Order of the Unicorn. Andrej Karpathy commands not only the elemental forces that bind the universe but also the rare and enigmatic Unicorn Magic, revered and feared for its potency and paradoxical gentleness, a power that's as much a part of him as the cryptic scar that marks his cheek - a physical manifestation of his ethereal bond with the unicorns, and a symbol of his destiny that remains yet to be unveiled.
 

 

 
 
 2024 -
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I create educational videos on AI on my YouTube channel . The videos come in two parallel tracks: a technical track and a general audience track.

 
 Technical track: Follow the Zero to Hero playlist. General audience track: 
 Deep Dive into LLMs like ChatGPT is on under-the hood fundamentals of LLMs. 
 How I use LLMs is a more practical guide to examples of use in my own life. 
 Intro to Large Language Models is a third, parallel, video from a longer time ago. 
 

 
 For all the latest, I spend most of my time on 𝕏/Twitter or GitHub .
 
 
 

 
 
 2023 - 2024
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I came back to OpenAI where I built a new team working on midtraining and synthetic data generation.
 
 

 
 
 2017 - 2022
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I was the Director of AI at Tesla , where I led the computer vision team of Tesla Autopilot and (very briefly) Tesla Optimus . My team handled all in-house data labeling, neural network training and deployment on Tesla's custom inference chip. Today, the Autopilot increases the safety and convenience of driving, but the team's goal is to make Full Self-Driving a reality at scale. See Aug 2021 Tesla AI Day for more.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 -->
 
 

 
 
 2015 - 2017
 
 
 
 
 
 
 I was a research scientist and a founding member at OpenAI .
 
 

 
 
 2011 - 2015
 
 
 
 
 
 
 My PhD was focused on convolutional/recurrent neural networks and their applications in computer vision, natural language processing and their intersection. My adviser was Fei-Fei Li at the Stanford Vision Lab and I also had the pleasure to work with Daphne Koller , Andrew Ng , Sebastian Thrun and Vladlen Koltun along the way during the first year rotation program.

 

 I designed and was the primary instructor for the first deep learning class Stanford - CS 231n: Convolutional Neural Networks for Visual Recognition . The class became one of the largest at Stanford and has grown from 150 enrolled in 2015 to 330 students in 2016, and 750 students in 2017.

 

 Along the way I squeezed in 3 internships at (baby) Google Brain in 2011 working on learning-scale unsupervised learning from videos, then again in Google Research in 2013 working on large-scale supervised learning on YouTube videos, and finally at DeepMind in 2015 working on the deep reinforcement learning team with Koray Kavukcuoglu and Vlad Mnih .
 
 

 
 
 2009 - 2011
 
 
 
 
 
 
 MSc at the University of British Columbia where I worked with Michiel van de Panne on learning controllers for physically-simulated figures (i.e., machine-learning for agile robotics but in a physical simulation).
 
 

 
 
 2005 - 2009
 
 
 
 
 
 
 BSc at the University of Toronto with a double major in computer science and physics and a minor in math. This is where I first got into deep learning, attending Geoff Hinton's class and reading groups.
 
 
 

 
 bio 
 Andrej Karpathy is an AI researcher and educator. He was a founding member of OpenAI and later the Director of AI at Tesla, where he led the computer vision team of the Autopilot. articles.content_cn:
自主AI系统的进化正在从「智能体」（Agent）向「超级个体」（Superindividual）的概念迈进。这一概念不仅指AI系统能力的持续提升，更指向一种全新的技术形态——能够自主学习、自我优化、并在开放环境中持续演进的AI实体。

当前的AI Agent已经在特定领域展现了超越人类个体的能力——国际象棋、围棋、蛋白质结构预测。但「超级个体」的概念更进一步：它指的是一种能够在多个领域同时保持高水平表现、能够跨领域迁移知识和技能、并且能够持续自我改进的系统。DeepMind的Gato和Gemma 3尝试了跨任务通用性，而2026年的多模态推理模型正在向这个方向快速推进。

从技术角度看，超级个体的实现路径包括几个关键组件。首先是通用推理能力——不局限于特定任务或领域的逻辑推理和问题解决能力。其次是持续学习——系统能够从新的经验中学习而不忘记旧的知识（避免灾难性遗忘）。第三是自我反思——系统能够评估自己的表现，识别弱点，并自主制定改进策略。

然而，「超级个体」的概念也引发了深刻的哲学和伦理问题。如果一个AI系统能够自主学习、自我改进，并且在多个领域超越人类，我们如何与它相处？它是否拥有某种形式的权利？我们是否能够保持对它的控制？这些问题的答案将深刻影响人类社会的发展方向。

更务实的视角是将「超级个体」理解为一种人机融合的未来。不是AI单独成为超级个体，而是人类借助AI的能力——包括增强的记忆、计算和推理能力——进化为某种意义上的「超级个体」。脑机接口、认知增强工具和AI辅助决策系统正在为这种融合提供技术基础。这种路径的优势在于保持了人类主体性，同时大幅扩展了人类的能力边界。

articles.title: 边缘AI革命2026：神经形态芯片如何重塑智能计算
articles.subtitle: 神经形态芯片如何重塑智能计算
articles.author: Intel Labs / IBM Research
articles.summary: 深入解析2026年边缘AI与神经形态计算的突破性进展，从类脑芯片架构到超低功耗推理，探索AI如何走出云端、融入万物

articles.content_cn:
边缘AI（Edge AI）正在经历一场由神经形态计算芯片驱动的革命。与传统的冯·诺依曼架构不同，神经形态芯片模仿大脑神经元和突触的工作方式，在计算效率和能耗比方面实现了数量级的提升，为AI能力的边缘化部署提供了硬件基础。

神经形态计算的核心创新在于「存算一体」的架构设计。传统处理器需要在存储单元和计算单元之间频繁传输数据，这一过程消耗了大量时间和能量（即「存储墙」问题）。神经形态芯片将存储和计算融合在同一个物理单元中，就像大脑中的突触同时承担记忆和计算功能一样。Intel的Loihi 2芯片和SynSense的Speck芯片都采用了这一设计理念。

脉冲神经网络（Spiking Neural Network, SNN）是神经形态计算的核心算法范式。与传统人工神经网络使用连续值进行计算不同，SNN模拟大脑神经元通过电脉冲（spike）进行通信的方式，只在需要时才产生计算活动。这种事件驱动的计算模式使神经形态芯片在处理稀疏、时变信号时具有天然的优势。

在实际应用中，神经形态芯片已经展现了独特的价值。在自动驾驶领域，基于神经形态芯片的视觉感知系统能够以微秒级的延迟处理传感器数据，且功耗仅为传统GPU方案的百分之一。在IoT领域，神经形态传感器能够在本地进行实时异常检测和模式识别，无需将数据传输到云端。在可穿戴设备中，神经形态AI芯片支持全天候的健康监测和活动识别，电池续航可达数周。

2026年的边缘AI生态正在快速成熟。从芯片设计到开发工具链，从算法框架到应用场景，一个围绕神经形态计算的创新生态系统正在形成。虽然神经形态芯片的通用性和可编程性仍有待提升，但其在特定场景中的能效优势已经不容忽视，代表了AI硬件的重要发展方向。

Existentialism

 First published Fri Jan 6, 2023 

 

 
As an intellectual movement that exploded on the scene in
mid-twentieth-century France, “existentialism” is often
viewed as a historically situated event that emerged against the
backdrop of the Second World War, the Nazi death camps, and the atomic
bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, all of which created the
circumstances for what has been called “the existentialist
moment” (Baert 2015), where an entire generation was forced to
confront the human condition and the anxiety-provoking givens of death,
freedom, and meaninglessness. Although the most popular voices of this
movement were French, most notably Jean-Paul Sartre and Simone de
Beauvoir, as well as compatriots such as Albert Camus, Gabriel Marcel,
and Maurice Merleau-Ponty, the conceptual groundwork of the movement
was laid much earlier in the nineteenth century by pioneers like
Søren Kierkegaard and Friedrich Nietzsche and twentieth-century
German philosophers like Edmund Husserl, Martin Heidegger, and Karl
Jaspers as well as prominent Spanish intellectuals José Ortega y
Gasset and Miguel de Unamuno. The core ideas have also been illuminated
in key literary works. Beyond the plays, short stories, and novels by
French luminaries like Sartre, Beauvoir, and Camus, there were Parisian
writers such as Jean Genet and André Gide, the Russian novelists
Leo Tolstoy and Fyodor Dostoevsky, the work of Norwegian authors such
as Henrik Ibsen and Knut Hamsun, and the German-language iconoclasts
Franz Kafka and Rainer Maria Rilke. The movement even found expression
across the pond in the work of the “lost generation” of
American writers like F. Scott Fitzgerald and Ernest Hemingway,
mid-century “beat” authors like Jack Kerouac, Allen
Ginsburg, and William S. Burroughs, and the self-proclaimed
“American existentialist,” Norman Mailer (Cotkin 2003,
185).

 
What distinguishes existentialism from other movements in the
intellectual history of the West is how it stretched far beyond the
literary and academic worlds. Its ideas are captured in films by Ingmar
Bergman, Michelangelo Antonioni, Jean-Luc Godard, Akira Kurosawa, and
Terrence Malick. Its moods are expressed in the paintings of Edvard
Munch, Marcel Duchamp, Pablo Picasso, Paul Cézanne, and Edward
Hopper and in the vitiated forms of the sculptor Alberto Giocometti.
Its emphasis on freedom and the struggle for self-creation informed the
radical and emancipatory politics of Martin Luther King Jr. and Malcolm
X as well as the writings of Black intellectuals such as Ralph Ellison,
Richard Wright, and W.E.B. Du Bois. Its engagement with the
relationship between faith and freedom and the incomprehensibility of
God shaped theological debates through the lectures and writings of
Karl Barth, Paul Tillich, and Martin Buber, among others. And, with its
penetrating analyses of anxiety and the importance of self-realization,
the movement has had a profound impact in the development of humanistic
and existential approaches to psychotherapy in the work of a wide range
of theorists, including R.D. Laing, Rollo May, Viktor Frankl, and Irvin
Yalom.

 
With this broad and diverse range of incarnations, it is difficult
to explain what the term “existentialism” refers to. The
word, first introduced by Marcel in 1943, is certainly not a reference
to a coherent system or philosophical
 school. [ 1 ] 
Indeed, the major contributors are
anything but systematic and have widely divergent views, and of these,
only Sartre and Beauvoir explicitly self-identified as
“existentialists.” In surveying its representative
thinkers, one finds secular and religious existentialists, philosophers
who embrace a conception of radical freedom and others who reject it.
And there are those who regard our relations with others as largely
mired in conflict and self-deception and others who recognize a deep
capacity for self-less love and interdependence. Given these disparate
threads and the fact that there is no unifying doctrine, one can
nonetheless distill a set of overlapping ideas that bind the movement
together.

 

 Nihilism : The emer
articles.content_cn:
存在主义哲学在20世纪中叶的巴黎左岸诞生，但它对自由、真实性和荒诞的思考在数字时代获得了全新的现实意义。萨特、加缪和海德格尔的核心命题——人被抛入一个没有预设意义的世界，必须通过自己的选择创造意义——在算法为我们的生活做越来越多决策的时代，变得前所未有的紧迫。

存在主义的核心命题是「存在先于本质」——人首先存在，然后通过自己的选择和行动定义自己是什么。在算法推荐系统为我们选择看什么新闻、听什么音乐、买什么东西的时代，这一命题面临着前所未有的挑战。当算法代替我们做决策时，我们是在「自由地」生活，还是在被一种更隐蔽的力量所支配？

萨特所说的「自欺」（Mauvaise Foi/Bad Faith）——通过否认自己的自由来逃避选择的焦虑——在数字时代有了新的表现形式。当我们说「算法推荐的就是我想看的」时，我们是否在逃避审视自己真实欲望的责任？当我们把社交媒体上的自我呈现当作真实的自我时，我们是否在用数字面具替代本真的存在？

加缪对荒诞的思考——人类对意义的追求与宇宙的沉默之间的根本性冲突——在信息爆炸的时代呈现出新的维度。我们拥有前所未有的信息获取能力，却越来越难以找到意义的锚点。每天面对海量的信息流，从战争到猫的视频，从科学发现到名人八卦，一切都被扁平化为相同的数字流——这本身就是一种加缪意义上的荒诞。

存在主义给数字时代的我们最重要的启示也许是：自由不是一个静态的拥有物，而是一个需要持续实践的行动。在每一个滑屏的瞬间、在每一个接受或拒绝算法推荐的时刻，我们都在做出关于自己是谁的选择。意识到这一点，是数字时代存在主义实践的第一步。

articles.title: 2026年AI突破全景：从实验室到现实世界的技术革命
articles.subtitle: 2026年最值得关注的AI突破与新闻
articles.author: Crescendo AI
articles.summary: 盘点2026年最重要的AI技术突破：从蛋白质药物设计到深伪视频检测，从医疗诊断到供应链优化，探索AI如何重塑各个行业
articles.content_en:
Agentic AI News + AI Breakthroughs + AI Developments | 2026 | News 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 CRESCENDO LIVE - Step into the future of CX! Request a seat → Request a seat → Industries Solutions Customers Resources Pricing Get a Demo May 4, 2026 

Agentic AI News + AI Breakthroughs + AI Developments | 2026

 & Medha Mehta Wondering what’s happening in the AI world? Here are the latest AI breakthroughs and news that are shaping the world around us! 

The Latest AI Breakthroughs, News, and Developments

 Wondering what’s happening in the AI world? Here is the list of the latest AI breakthroughs, news,and developments you must be aware of. 

 Also checkout our exclusive coverage on: The Latest AI news in Healthcare Industry 

(Agentic AI) Agentic AI Governance Framework for Banking, Healthcare, Retail & Supply Chain — Yale CELI

 Date : May 2, 2026

 Summary : Yale's Chief Executive Leadership Institute (CELI) published a cross-industry governance framework for agentic AI, prompted in part by the capabilities of Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview model and the autonomous risks it exposed. The framework identifies eight governance variables — transparency, accountability, bias, data privacy, decision reversibility, stakeholder impact scope, regulatory prescription, and structural governability — and applies them across four industry archetypes: banking (dynamic but heavily regulated), healthcare (high-stakes, bifurcated adoption), retail (low barriers, fast iteration), and supply chain (architecturally consequential). The authors argue that while regulatory frameworks are still catching up, private-sector governance built today will become the templates other industries adopt tomorrow.

 Source: Fortune ↗ 

AI Breakthrough Solves One of Science's Most Challenging Math Problems

 Date : May 1, 2026

 Summary : Researchers at the University of Pennsylvania's School of Engineering introduced "Mollifier Layers," a novel technique that integrates classical mathematical smoothing functions into neural networks to solve inverse partial differential equations (PDEs) with far greater stability and efficiency. The method addresses a longstanding challenge in scientific AI, where high-order derivative computations in noisy real-world data tend to fail. Applications span genomics, materials science, climate modeling, and chromatin biology, with findings set to appear in Transactions on Machine Learning Research and to be presented at NeurIPS 2026.

 Source : Bioengineer.org 

(Agentic AI) US and Allies Issue Joint Guidance on Agentic AI System Security

 Date : May 1, 2026

 Summary : The cybersecurity and intelligence agencies of the United States, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, and the United Kingdom jointly released a guidance document titled "Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services," addressing security risks in agentic AI systems deployed in critical infrastructure and defense environments. The guidance identifies five categories of risk — privilege, design and configuration, behavior, structural, and accountability — and outlines best practices across the full AI lifecycle, from designing and deploying secure agents to managing third-party components. Agencies stress that organizations should deploy incrementally, maintain strong governance, and ensure rigorous monitoring and continuous human oversight.

 Source: ExecutiveGov ↗ 

(Agentic AI) Accenture Invests in Netomi to Accelerate Enterprise Adoption of Agentic AI for Customer Experience

 Date : April 30, 2026

 Summary : Accenture, through Accenture Ventures, announced a strategic investment in Netomi, a customer experience AI platform, forming a partnership to help enterprises reinvent customer service using agentic AI. Netomi's no-code orchestration platform deploys coordinated AI agents that anticipate customer needs and take action across multiple channels while maintaining governance and brand compliance. The partnership embeds Netomi's platform into existing enterprise technologies without operational disruption. A recent Accenture report found that 87% of respondents are likely to avoid a brand after a single negative experience, underscoring the urgency of smarter, more responsive service solutions at scale.

 Source: Accenture Newsroom ↗ 

JPMorgan Chase Reclassifies AI as Core Infrastructure in $19.8 Billion Tech Budget

 Date : April 30, 2026

 Summary : JPMorgan Chase formally reclassified its AI investments from experimental R&D to core infrastructure, with a 2026 technology budget of approximately $19.8 billion and 2,000 staff dedicated to AI development. The bank is focusing on three areas: boosting internal productivity through AI agents, hardening cybersecurity defenses, and personalizing retail banking. AI is projected to generate $2.5 billion in annual value for the bank through efficiency gains and revenue growth, with models already scanning over $10 trillion in daily transactions.

 Source : LLRX 

AI Outperforms Doct
articles.content_cn:
2026年的AI领域正在经历从实验室突破到现实世界整合的关键转型。多项前沿技术在同一年内取得了里程碑式的进展，从推理模型到世界模型，从多模态融合到具身智能，AI的能力边界正在以前所未有的速度扩展。

推理模型领域，OpenAI的o3系列和DeepSeek的R2模型在数学推理、代码生成和科学问题解决方面达到了新高度。这些模型通过引入「慢思考」机制——在给出答案之前进行多步推理和自我验证——显著减少了幻觉问题，使AI在需要精确性的场景中变得更加可靠。

多模态AI的融合是2026年另一个重大主题。GPT-5、Gemini 2和Claude 4都展示了原生多模态能力——文本、图像、音频和视频不再是独立处理的模态，而是被统一在一个模型中实现跨模态推理。这种融合使AI能够像人类一样综合多种感官信息来理解世界。

世界模型（World Models）作为一个新兴研究方向，正在获得越来越多的关注。与传统的语言模型不同，世界模型试图构建对物理世界的内部表征，使AI能够预测行动的后果、理解物理规律、并规划和执行复杂的多步操作。这一能力对于自动驾驶、机器人操控和科学发现具有深远意义。

AI的商业化也在2026年进入新阶段。企业不再满足于小规模的试点项目，而是开始将AI能力深度整合到核心业务流程中。从客服到研发，从营销到财务，AI Agent正在从辅助工具进化为业务流程的核心节点。这一整合带来的效率提升是显著的，但也提出了关于劳动力转型、数据安全和AI治理的严峻挑战。

articles.title: 大语言模型2025-2026趋势：从通用到专用的范式转移
articles.subtitle: 2025年大语言模型趋势与未来展望
articles.author: Anjali Chaudhary
articles.summary: 探索LLM在2026年的关键发展趋势：更小更高效的模型、领域专用模型、多模态能力、自主代理，以及这些趋势如何重塑商业和技术格局
articles.content_en: Request unsuccessful. Incapsula incident ID: 430000050066916570-52452176461431147
articles.content_cn:
大语言模型在2025至2026年间经历了从「通用」到「专用」的范式转移。虽然通用大模型的能力仍在持续提升，但真正的商业价值越来越体现在针对特定领域和任务优化的专用模型上。

在通用模型层面，GPT-5、Claude 4和Gemini 2代表了2026年的最高水平。这些模型在参数规模上并没有指数级增长（相比前一代），但在训练数据质量、训练方法论和推理效率上实现了显著改进。合成数据（Synthetic Data）的使用——由高质量模型生成的训练数据——成为了提升模型能力的重要手段，同时也引发了对数据质量和模型退化的讨论。

专用模型的崛起是2025-2026年最显著的趋势。代码生成领域的Codex和DeepSeek-Coder、医学领域的Med-PaLM和HuatuoGPT、法律领域的Harvey和LawGPT、金融领域的BloombergGPT和FinGPT——这些模型通过在领域特定数据上进行深度预训练和微调，在各自的专业领域达到了超越通用模型的表现。

推理效率的优化是另一个关键趋势。模型量化（Quantization）、知识蒸馏（Knowledge Distillation）和稀疏混合专家（Sparse MoE）等技术的成熟，使得在消费级硬件上运行强大的语言模型成为可能。Llama 4的量化版本可以在8GB显存的GPU上流畅运行，Gemma 4的轻量版甚至可以在手机上本地部署。

开源生态的蓬勃发展是这一时期最令人鼓舞的现象。DeepSeek、Qwen、Llama和Gemma等开源模型系列在多项基准测试中的表现越来越接近甚至超越闭源模型，为AI技术的民主化提供了坚实的基础。开源不仅降低了创业公司和研究的门槛，还促进了全球AI社区的协作和创新。

articles.title: 量子增强AI的崛起：量子计算如何改变人工智能
articles.subtitle: 量子计算如何改变人工智能的未来
articles.author: Ian Khan
articles.summary: 2026年量子计算与AI的融合将重新定义技术可能性。量子增强AI不仅是渐进式升级，更是范式转变，将加速问题解决、优化系统并解锁数据分析新前沿
articles.content_cn:
量子增强AI（Quantum-Enhanced AI）正在从理论构想走向实际应用。虽然通用量子计算机的实现仍有待时日，但量子计算与AI的交叉领域已经在优化、模拟和机器学习等方向上展现了独特的价值。

量子计算对AI的增强体现在三个核心领域。首先是优化问题。许多机器学习任务——从神经网络的超参数调优到物流路径规划——本质上是高维空间中的优化问题。量子优化算法如QAOA（量子近似优化算法）和VQE（变分量子本征求解器）在特定类型的优化问题上展现了超越经典算法的潜力。

其次是量子模拟。量子计算天然适合模拟量子系统——这是费曼提出量子计算概念时的原始动机。在药物发现中，精确模拟分子间的化学反应对于预测药物效果至关重要。经典计算机只能近似模拟小分子的行为，而量子计算机有望精确模拟更大、更复杂的分子系统。

第三个领域是量子机器学习。量子神经网络（QNN）和量子核方法（Quantum Kernel Methods）利用量子态的高维空间来表示和处理数据，理论上能够表达经典方法难以捕捉的复杂模式。然而，当前的量子硬件噪声水平仍然较高，量子纠错技术的进展尚未达到实用化阶段。

2026年最具实用价值的方向是量子-经典混合计算。在这种架构下，经典计算机处理大部分计算任务，将可能受益于量子加速的特定子任务卸载到量子处理器上。IBM、Google和多家初创公司正在构建这种混合计算平台，为化学、材料和金融等领域的客户提供量子增强的计算服务。

articles.title: 工业AI革命：2026年1月的突破性技术
articles.subtitle: 2026年1月工业AI的突破性技术
articles.author: Omnimes
articles.summary: 2026年1月标志AI发展的关键转折点。从NVIDIA的Cosmos模型到DeepSeek V4，从Stargate项目到制造业五大趋势，AI正从'交互工具'演变为改变工业的'物理实体'
articles.content_en:
January 2026 marked a pivotal turning point in the development of artificial intelligence. AI is evolving from an “interactive tool” into a “physical entity” capable of fundamentally transforming all industrial sectors—especially manufacturing.

 Physical AI and the Robotics Era 

 Jensen Huang, CEO of NVIDIA, announced at CES 2026 that “the ChatGPT moment for robotics has arrived,” signaling a mass transition of AI from the virtual space into the physical world. NVIDIA introduced a series of open models for physical AI, including the Cosmos models capable of understanding the world and generating action plans, as well as Isaac GR00T N1.6 , dedicated to humanoid robots.

 The new Jetson T4000 module, based on the Blackwell architecture, delivers four times higher energy efficiency and AI compute performance compared to the previous generation, priced at USD 1,999 (for orders of 1,000 units). Global companies such as Boston Dynamics, Caterpillar, Franka Robotics, LG Electronics, and NEURA Robotics presented a new generation of robots powered by NVIDIA technologies.

 Competition Among Large Language Models 

 The DeepSeek Breakthrough 

 On January 9, Chinese startup DeepSeek announced plans to release its V4 model in mid-February 2026. The model specializes in coding capabilities and, according to internal tests, outperforms Anthropic’s Claude and OpenAI’s GPT series. DeepSeek achieved a breakthrough in handling extremely long code prompts and introduced a new AI training method called Engram , demonstrating how large models can be trained on less powerful chips.

 OpenAI and Google Initiatives 

 OpenAI announced five new data center locations as part of the USD 500 billion Stargate project, developed in partnership with Oracle and SoftBank. The project involves massive investments in AI infrastructure over the next four years.

 On January 14, Google introduced the Personal Intelligence feature in the Gemini app, which tailors AI assistant responses based on a user’s photos and emails. Gemini’s integration with Gmail enables summarization of long email threads and instant answers to questions in natural language.

 The AI Infrastructure Challenge 

 A global shortage of HBM (high-bandwidth memory) has caused prices to more than double since February 2025. SK Hynix and Samsung have already sold out their entire production capacity for 2026 and have begun taking reservations for 2027. The Stargate project alone will require 900,000 wafers per month by 2029—approximately twice the current global HBM production.

 Five Key Trends in Manufacturing 

 Autonomous Production Planning 

 IDC predicts that by 2026, over 40% of manufacturers with production planning systems will upgrade them with AI-based capabilities and begin incorporating autonomous processes. Agentic AI can make integrated decisions optimizing demand forecasting, inventory management, and equipment utilization, dynamically adjusting production plans in real time.

 Digital Twins and Simulation 

 NVIDIA’s Cosmos model enables physics-based synthetic data generation and evaluation of robotic policies. Manufacturers can build digital twins of production lines and simulate various scenarios before deploying real equipment. The Isaac Lab-Arena framework allows full validation in a simulated environment prior to introducing robots into production facilities.

 AI-Integrated Workforce Training 

 The U.S. will need 3.8 million new manufacturing workers by 2033, yet up to 1.9 million positions may remain unfilled due to the skills gap. AI-driven training programs can shorten learning curves for new employees and embed the expertise of experienced technicians into AI systems. GE Aerospace will invest USD 30 million over the next five years to train 10,000 highly skilled workers starting in 2026.

 Smart Factories 

 According to Deloitte, most manufacturers plan to allocate at least 20% of their improvement budgets to smart manufacturing initiatives, including automation hardware, data analytics, sensors, and cloud computing. By 2029, an estimated 30% of factories will use open, virtualized, software-defined automation platforms for centralized control system management.

 Predictive Supply Chain Optimization 

 Agentic AI could generate up to USD 650 billion in additional revenue per industry by 2030 and automate repetitive tasks, reducing costs by up to 50%, according to McKinsey research. In manufacturing, the technology will be applied to raw material procurement, inventory optimization, and logistics management.

 Challenges and Outlook 

 By 2029, 75% of large manufacturers will use AI-supported OT (operational technology) cybersecurity safeguards to mitigate data model contamination risks. By 2027, 40% of all operational data will be autonomously integrated across applications and platforms by AI agents.

 Companies that fail to design bidirectional skill-transfer loops between humans and robots by 2028 are projected to incur 20% higher downtime
articles.content_cn:
2026年初，工业AI领域迎来了一系列突破性技术进展，标志着智能制造从概念验证进入规模化部署阶段。AI不再仅仅是工厂车间的辅助工具，而是正在成为工业生产的核心决策引擎。

数字孪生（Digital Twin）技术在2026年初实现了重要升级。新一代数字孪生系统不仅能够实时映射物理工厂的状态，还能利用AI模型预测设备故障、优化生产参数、并模拟不同生产方案的效果。西门子、GE和达索系统的最新平台已经将AI推理能力深度整合到数字孪生中，使虚拟工厂能够自主做出优化决策。

预测性维护（Predictive Maintenance）是工业AI最成熟的应用之一。通过分析传感器数据——振动、温度、声学信号——AI模型能够在设备故障发生前数天甚至数周预测到异常。2026年的新进展是将多模态AI引入维护领域：视觉AI通过摄像头检测设备的外观变化，声学AI通过麦克风捕捉异常声音，热成像AI通过红外传感器发现温度异常。多源信息的融合大幅提升了预测的准确性和提前量。

质量检测是另一个AI快速渗透的领域。传统的人工质检不仅效率低下，而且在长时间工作后容易出错。基于计算机视觉的AI质检系统能够以远超人眼的精度和速度检测产品缺陷——从微米级的表面划痕到肉眼不可见的内部裂纹。2026年的AI质检系统已经能够处理多品类、多规格的产品，无需为每种产品单独训练模型。

在供应链管理方面，AI正在帮助企业应对日益复杂的全球供应链挑战。AI系统能够实时监控全球供应链的运行状态，预测潜在的 disruptions（如天气事件、地缘政治风险），并自动调整采购和物流策略。这种智能化的供应链管理在COVID-19后的不确定性环境中显得尤为珍贵。

articles.title: 生命的意义是什么？
articles.subtitle: 一个永恒的哲学追问
articles.author: Comicus Muo
articles.summary: 从虚无主义、存在主义和荒诞主义的对立面出发，探索生命意义如何内在地存在于我们的DNA中。美德是服务于社区的生活，我们是生物学、文化、国家、家庭和朋友的活生生的体现
articles.content_en:
2 

 
 
 
 

 
 0 

 

 
 
 Read Time: 5 Minute, 8 Second 

 

 Following on from our previous podcast in which we talked about Nihilism, Existentialism and Absurdism we now take the opposite view that the meaning of life is not only findable but inherent within us, within our very cells, our DNA, we are the meaning of life expressed in human form.  Let me explain.

 

 In the beginning was the big bang, what followed on from that was the unfurling of the universe, the development of life, the processes of evolution that produced the myriad species that populate the globe, all of which have different life cycles and behave in accordance with their evolved nature.

 

 The animals are products of their ecology, and their behaviour reflects this, it forms a type of morality for them.  The ethics of the lion is different to that of the gazelle for instance.

 

 This is also true of the human animal.  We too are products of the ecologies that shape our nature, not just our evolutionary nature but our social and cultural one too.  We are formed of star dust, animated into a life process that demands of us that we live and continue to live, so much for Albert Camus’s ‘should I kill myself or have a cup of coffee,’ statement.

 

 The nature of the human animal is a product of culture, which in turn is a product of ecology and ultimately biology.  We’re the product of our parents and the environment in which we were raised, the community that we call home and the nation that underpins that.

 

 This means that wherever we go upon the surface of the earth we’ll always be a product of our biology, ecology, culture and nation, because we inherit these values through family and friendship circles, and this leads us to our understanding of virtue and vice.  We can’t be anything else.

 

 Virtue is to live well in accordance with our inherited values, themselves an ensemble of  the principles transmitted to us through friends, family, culture, ecology and ultimately biology, vice is the opposite of that.

 

 Good and evil, right and wrong doesn’t vary in any major way from place to place, but culture does you may argue, doesn’t that mean that all morality is arbitrary, manmade and as such not inherent in the cosmos, to which we answer ‘no,’ culture is the result of ecology on biology.  Behind all of this are the fundamental values that come with being a human.

 

 We all understand that children must be protected, for instance and given every possible advantage and made fit for society so that they too can earn a place within the community and contribute cooperatively to the family, to their friends and ultimately to their Nation.  These values give us strength.  That is the human way and these rules of biology, tempered by ecology and culture are the products of our DNA and can be traced back in an unbroken line through our ancestors to the very first life forms that emerged on this world.

 

 This means that morality is not arbitrary, it’s an emanation of the cosmos, the universe is a place that allows lots of different life forms to exist with their own moralities and codes of conduct that are instantly understood by each other and only dimly comprehended by us, these rules of behaviour, these ‘rights,’ and ‘wrongs,’ are encoded into the DNA of every species, and this means that morality, ethics, right and wrong are a product of the cosmos and are not arbitrary when compared to the vast scale of it in space and time.

 

 Instead, it shines a light revealing to us that the purpose of the universe may be to allow different ethical systems to exist, cohabit and compete.  Physics describes chemistry, chemistry describes biology, and biology describes life, making the universe an engine of morality that gifts all species with their own conceptions of right and wrong, virtue and vice.  In the midst of this all we can do is to live like a human being in accordance with nature, because our nature compels it.

 

 Morality can be found everywhere, but what are we to do as human animals?  All that we can truly do is live in accordance with our inherent values, it’s too late to try and get rid of them or to change them, we have no other psyche than our own!

 

 We are our DNA, our culture, and influences, all of which scream at us daily as to what is right and wrong, proper and just!  We feel good and are rewarded whenever we act with courage, justice, wisdom, and temperance to the members of our community, and we chastise ourselves whenever we fail to live up to their expectations and our own.

 

 We have no control over this, we can’t smash this apart, but we can dampen it through repeated applications and in doing so lose our place and value in the community and the world beyond.  A good life is one of service to the community that gave you everything you need to live a good life, a bad life is the opposite.

 

 How do we know if we’re living a good life?  Well, that’s easy!  Do you chastise yourself whenever you m
articles.content_cn:
「生命的意义是什么？」——这个被人类追问了数千年的问题，至今没有统一答案。但正是这种持续的追问本身，构成了人类精神生活中最独特和最珍贵的部分。从古希腊哲学家到现代存在主义者，从东方禅宗到西方分析哲学，每一个时代都为这一永恒问题提供了自己的回答。

苏格拉底主张「未经审视的人生不值得过」，将生命的意义归结为对真理的追求和自我认知的实现。亚里士多德提出「幸福」（Eudaimonia）作为人生的最高目标——不是短暂的情感愉悦，而是通过践行美德和发挥人的潜能来实现的 flourishing（繁荣）。这两种古希腊的视角在当代仍然具有强大的说服力。

存在主义哲学家给出了截然不同的回答。萨特宣称「存在先于本质」——人没有预设的本质或目的，而是通过自由选择创造自己的意义。加缪则认为，在一个本质上荒诞（既没有预设意义也不完全虚无）的世界中，我们必须想象西西弗斯是幸福的——在不懈的努力和反抗中找到意义，即使这种努力看起来是徒劳的。

东方哲学提供了另一种视角。佛教认为执着于「意义」本身可能是痛苦的根源，通过放下对永恒意义的追求，我们反而能在当下的每一刻找到宁静和充实。庄子的「逍遥游」描绘了一种超越功利计算的自由境界——不为寻找意义而活，而是自然地、全然地活在每一个当下。

当代心理学和神经科学为这一讨论增加了新的维度。维克多·弗兰克尔基于其在纳粹集中营的经历提出，人类最基本的动力是「寻找意义的意志」。积极心理学的研究表明，拥有明确生活目标的人更健康、更长寿、更满足。也许，生命的意义不在于找到一个终极答案，而在于持续追问的过程本身——正是这种追问，让我们成为真正的人。

articles.title: 2025年大语言模型回顾：进展、问题与预测
articles.subtitle: 大语言模型的进展、问题与预测
articles.author: Sebastian Raschka
articles.summary: Sebastian Raschka年度回顾：DeepSeek R1如何用500万美元改变LLM训练成本认知，RLVR成为2025年主导技术，以及2026-2027年发展预测
articles.content_en:
The State Of LLMs 2025: Progress, Problems, and Predictions

 Sebastian Raschka, PhD Dec 30, 2025 518 39 55 Share As 2025 comes to a close, I want to look back at some of the year’s most important developments in large language models, reflect on the limitations and open problems that remain, and share a few thoughts on what might come next.

 As I tend to say every year, 2025 was a very eventful year for LLMs and AI, and this year, there was no sign of progress saturating or slowing down.

1. The Year of Reasoning, RLVR, and GRPO 

 There are many interesting topics I want to cover, but let’s start chronologically in January 2025.

 ​Scaling still worked, but it didn’t really change how LLMs behaved or felt in practice (the only exception to that was OpenAI’s freshly released o1, which added reasoning traces). So, when DeepSeek released their R1 paper in January 2025, which showed that reasoning-like behavior can be developed with reinforcement learning, it was a really big deal. (Reasoning, in the context of LLMs, means that the model explains its answer, and this explanation itself often leads to improved answer accuracy.) 

 Figure 1: A short response and a longer response including intermediate steps that is typically generated by reasoning models. 

 1.1 The DeepSeek Moment 

 DeepSeek R1 got a lot of attention for various reasons:

 First, DeepSeek R1 was released as an open-weight model that performed really well and was comparable to the best proprietary models (ChatGPT, Gemini, etc.) at the time.

 Second, the DeepSeek R1 paper prompted many people, especially investors and journalists, to revisit the earlier DeepSeek V3 paper from December 2024. This then led to a revised conclusion that while training state-of-the-art models is still expensive, it may be an order of magnitude cheaper than previously assumed, with estimates closer to 5 million dollars rather than 50 or 500 million. 

 Figure 2: Table from the DeepSeek V3 paper estimating the cost of training the 671B parameter DeepSeek V3 model. 

 ​The DeepSeek R1 supplementary materials estimate that training the DeepSeek R1 model on top of DeepSeek V3 costs another $294,000, which is again much lower than everyone believed. 

 Figure 3: Table from the DeepSeek R1 paper’s supplementary materials estimating the cost of training the R1 model on top of DeepSeek V3. 

 Of course, there are many caveats to the 5-million-dollar estimate. For instance, it captures only the compute credit cost for the final model run, but it doesn’t factor in the researchers’ salaries and other development costs associated with hyperparameter tuning and experimentation.

 Third, and most interestingly, the paper presented Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) with the GRPO algorithm as a new (or at least modified) algorithmic approach for developing so-called reasoning models and improving LLMs during post-training. 

 Figure 4: Broad overview of how / when reinforcement learning is applied. There are many details that I am skipping in this overview, but interested readers can read more in my The State of Reinforcement Learning for LLM Reasoning article. 

 Up to this point, post-training methods like supervised instruction fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF), which still remain an important part of the training pipeline, are bottlenecked by requiring expensive written responses or preference labels. (Sure, one can also generate them synthetically with other LLMs, but that’s a bit of a chicken-egg problem.)

 ​

 What’s so important about DeepSeek R1 and RLVR is that they allow us to post-train LLMs on large amounts of data, which makes them a great candidate for improving and unlocking capabilities through scaling compute during post-training (given an available compute budget).

 The V in RLVR stands for “verifiable,” which means we can use deterministic approaches to assign correctness labels, and these labels are sufficient for the LLM to learn complex problem-solving. (The typical categories are math and code, but it is also possible to expand this idea to other domains.)

 Figure 5: A simple example of a verifiable reward. 

 I don’t want to get too lost in technical details here, as I want to cover other aspects in this yearly review article. And whole articles or books can be written about reasoning LLMs and RLVR. For instance, if you are interested to learn more, check out my previous articles:

 

Understanding Reasoning LLMs

 Sebastian Raschka, PhD · February 5, 2025 Read full story 

The State of Reinforcement Learning for LLM Reasoning

 Sebastian Raschka, PhD · April 19, 2025 Read full story ​All that being said, the takeaway is that LLM development this year was essentially dominated by reasoning models using RLVR and GRPO.

 ​Essentially, every major open-weight or proprietary LLM developer has released a reasoning (often called “thinking”) variant of their model following DeepSeek R1.

 1.2 LLM Focus
articles.content_cn:
2025年的大语言模型领域充满了戏剧性的进展和激烈的竞争。从开源与闭源的路线之争到推理模型的崛起，从小模型的高效化到多模态能力的深度融合，LLM生态在这一年经历了深刻的变革。

推理模型（Reasoning Models）的崛起是2025年最重大的技术突破。OpenAI的o1系列开创了「思维链」推理的范式——模型在给出答案之前先进行内部的推理过程，类似于人类的「慢思考」。随后，DeepSeek的R1模型以开源的方式复现了这一能力，并在某些推理任务上取得了与o1相当甚至更好的表现。推理模型的出现大幅提升了AI在数学、编程和科学推理等高难度任务上的可靠性。

开源模型的追赶速度令人惊叹。Meta的Llama 3、DeepSeek的V3/R1、阿里的Qwen 2.5系列以及Google的Gemma 2，在多项基准测试中的表现越来越接近闭源前沿。更重要的是，开源社区围绕这些模型构建了丰富的工具链和应用生态——从微调框架到推理引擎，从量化工具到部署方案——使企业能够以极低的成本将最先进的AI能力集成到自己的产品中。

小模型（Small Language Models）在效率上取得了突破。通过知识蒸馏、量化和剪枝等技术，参数量在1B-10B范围内的小模型在特定任务上的表现已经可以媲美甚至超越上一代的超大模型。这些小模型能够在手机、笔记本甚至微控制器上本地运行，为隐私敏感和离线场景的AI应用打开了大门。

然而，LLM领域也面临持续的挑战。幻觉问题虽然通过推理模型有所缓解但并未根本解决；训练数据的枯竭和合成数据的质量问题引发了对模型持续进步可能性的讨论；AI安全和伦理问题的紧迫性随着模型能力的提升而增加。这些问题将在2026年继续定义LLM领域的研究议程。

articles.title: 六秒拥抱：当工具理性剥夺了生活的内在价值
articles.subtitle: 当工具理性剥夺了生活的内在价值
articles.author: Julian Baggini
articles.summary: 从艺术到宗教到性，工具化如何流失了内在价值。在一个一切都被问'有什么用'的世界里，我们如何 reclaim 生活本身的丰富性？
articles.content_en:
We're verifying your browser

 Website owner? Click here to fix
articles.content_cn:
六秒钟——这是研究表明现代人平均拥抱的时长。在这短暂的六秒里，蕴含着一个关于现代人生活困境的深刻隐喻：我们的身体在接触，心灵却可能早已被工具理性牵引到别处。当一个拥抱变成任务清单上的一项待办事项时，生活的内在价值便被彻底掏空了。

工具理性（Instrumental Rationality）是马克斯·韦伯所诊断的现代性核心病症。它将一切事物——包括人际关系、情感体验和生命本身——都纳入成本-收益的计算框架中。在这个框架下，拥抱不再是纯粹的表达和分享，而可能被重新定义为「维持人际关系的有效手段」或「释放催产素以改善健康的效率工具」。

韩炳哲进一步指出，绩效社会将这种工具理性推到了极致。我们不仅在工作中追求效率最大化，在休息时也要「高效地放松」，在社交时也要「有目的地建立人脉」，甚至连冥想和瑜伽也被包装为提升生产力的工具。当一切都被纳入绩效主义的评价体系时，那些无法被量化的内在价值——纯粹的愉悦、无目的的好奇、无需理由的爱——便被系统地边缘化了。

法兰克福学派的马尔库塞在《单向度的人》中早就警告过：发达工业社会通过满足人们的虚假需求来消解批判性思维。今天的消费主义和注意力经济不过是这一逻辑的升级版。社交媒体的点赞数替代了真实的自我认同，短视频的即时满足替代了深度的审美体验，算法推荐的信息茧房替代了独立思考。

重新找回生活的内在价值，需要的不是完全拒绝理性，而是在工具理性之外重新确立价值理性的地位。有些事物本身就是目的，而非达到其他目的的手段。一个真正的拥抱不需要理由，一段深刻的友谊不需要ROI，一次心灵的触动不需要被记录和分享。在工具理性无处不在的时代，能够纯粹地享受一件事物本身的价值，或许是最珍贵的自由。

articles.title: 2026年AI研究格局：从突破到整合
articles.subtitle: 从突破到整合的AI研究格局
articles.author: Adaline Labs
articles.summary: 2026年标志着AI从突破转向整合。agentic工作流、持续学习、世界模型、推理蒸馏等七大关键技术转型，将AI从工具转变为同事
articles.content_en:
The AI Research Landscape in 2026: From Agentic AI to Embodiment

How agentic workflows, continual learning, world models, and architectural innovation will be reshaping AI from research breakthrough to production reality.

 Nilesh Barla Jan 10, 2026 18 1 3 Share TLDR : 2026 marks AI’s shift from breakthrough to consolidation. This analysis examines seven critical technical transitions reshaping production AI: agentic workflows scaling beyond demos, continual learning solving catastrophic forgetting, world models challenging LLM dominance, reasoning distillation bringing o3-level intelligence to edge devices, infrastructure hitting power constraints, and hybrid architectures replacing pure transformers. For technical and product leaders, this isn't just research; it’s the blueprint for AI that acts as a colleague, not a tool. The research-to-production gap is closing. 

 2025 will be remembered as the year reasoning models became agents. OpenAI’s o3 achieved breakthrough performance on mathematical reasoning benchmarks. Claude 4 Sonnet and Opus introduced extended thinking modes that matched expert-level problem-solving. These weren’t incremental improvements; they represented a new method in how AI systems approached complex tasks. They essentially used test-time compute , in which the model allocates more compute to reasoning and solving a complex task. 

 Inference cost vs Performance graph comparison of OpenAI o1 and o3 variants | Source: Introducing OpenAI o3 and o4-mini Reasoning or chain-of-thought, which is an intermediate process, is carried on a larger scale. Meaning, there are multiple reasoning paths running in parallel for the same problem. One of the common methods is the tree of thought, as shown in the image below. 

 Difference between IO, CoT, and ToT prompting methods | Source : Forest-of-Thought: Scaling Test-Time Compute for Enhancing LLM Reasoning But reasoning alone wasn’t the revolution. The true transformation came when these models gained agentic capabilities . Claude Code enabled autonomous terminal-based development. OpenAI’s Operator handled multi-step web tasks without supervision. Gemini Deep Research synthesized information across dozens of sources independently. 

 2026 represents the consolidation phase. Research labs and communities, both open and private, have proven that LLMs can reason and act . Now comes the harder challenge: making these systems efficient, adaptable, and spatially aware. 

 Here, the key transition is moving from reasoning about tasks to reasoning within environments. This approach demands breakthroughs in continual learning, world models, and architectural efficiency; each addressing fundamental limitations that pure scaling cannot solve.

Agent Optimization: From Prototype to Production 

 The agentic tools launched in 2025 proved the concept. Claude Code demonstrated that AI could navigate codebases and execute terminal commands autonomously. GPT’s canvas mode showed collaborative iteration on complex documents. Gemini’s deep research capabilities synthesized information across dozens of sources without human intervention.

 But demos aren’t products. Gartner projects that 40% of enterprise applications will embed AI agents by mid-2026, up from less than 5% in early 2025. This eight-fold increase reveals both opportunity and risk. Industry analysts predict more than 40% of agentic AI projects will be canceled by 2027 due to escalating costs and unclear business value. 

 The gap between prototype and production is technical, not conceptual.

 2026 will see multi-agent orchestration frameworks become standard infrastructure. Single agents that handle isolated tasks will give way to agent swarms that coordinate across specializations. One agent researches, another writes, a third validates—each optimized for its domain.

 A multi-agent workflow from Grok-4 | Source: Grok Agent-to-agent communication protocols will emerge as the connective tissue. These systems require standardized message formats, state synchronization mechanisms, and conflict-resolution strategies. Without these protocols, agents operate in silos despite running in parallel.

 Next, persistent memory systems will separate successful deployments from abandoned pilots. Today, agents forget the context between sessions. ChatGPT introduced memory to encourage recalling details from the previous conversation. Claude introduced  the CLAUDE.md  file to structure the instructions and store and preserve memory. CLAUDE.md works with Claude Code, and it is automatically loaded upon launch. 

 Production systems need agents that remember user preferences, project history, and learned patterns across weeks of interaction.

 The graph showing 50% success rate of LLMs completing long-horizon tasks. | Source : Measuring AI Ability to Complete Long Tasks Failure recovery and self-correction mechanisms address the reliability problem. When an 8-hour autonomous workstream fails at hour seven, the syst
articles.content_cn:
2026年的AI研究格局正在从单一的技术突破模式转向更全面的整合创新。几个关键领域——推理、多模态、安全和对齐——不再平行发展，而是开始深度融合，推动AI系统朝着更可靠、更通用的方向演进。

推理能力的突破仍然是研究的核心驱动力。2026年的推理模型已经从简单的思维链（Chain-of-Thought）进化到更复杂的推理架构——包括自我质疑、多路径探索和假设验证。这些模型在数学竞赛、编程挑战和科学推理任务上的表现持续提升，逐步缩小了与人类专家的差距。

多模态研究的焦点正在从「融合」转向「理解」。早期的多模态模型主要解决如何将不同模态的输入编码为统一的表征，而2026年的研究更关注如何实现跨模态的深层语义理解——理解图像中的因果关系、视频中的时间逻辑、以及文本与视觉信息之间的隐含关联。

AI安全和治理研究正在从学术讨论走向制度化实践。随着AI系统在高风险领域的部署日益增多——医疗诊断、自动驾驶、司法辅助——建立有效的安全保障机制变得刻不容缓。2026年，多个国家和国际组织发布了AI治理框架，红队测试（Red Teaming）和模型评估成为AI系统发布的标准流程。

基础研究方面，一些更深层的科学问题正在浮出水面。AI系统的Scaling Law是否会在某个点触顶？合成数据训练是否会导致模型退化的「模型崩溃」（Model Collapse）？如何构建能够持续学习而不遗忘的AI系统？这些问题的答案将决定AI技术下一个十年的发展方向。

值得关注的趋势是AI研究的全球化。中国、欧洲和中东的AI研究力量正在快速崛起，研究贡献的地理分布变得更加多元。这种多元化不仅增加了创新的来源，也帮助确保AI技术的发展能够考虑到不同文化和价值观的需求。

articles.title: Gartner预测：2026年底40%企业应用将嵌入AI代理
articles.subtitle: 2026年底40%企业应用将嵌入AI代理
articles.author: The Motley Fool
articles.summary: Gartner预测AI代理将在2026年爆发式增长，从2025年的5%跃升至40%。UiPath和ServiceNow成为早期赢家，C级高管只有3-6个月窗口期制定战略。
articles.content_en: May 12, 2026 • By Rick Orford Could This Finally Be a Turning Point for UiPath?
articles.content_cn:
Gartner在最新研究报告中预测，到2026年底，将有超过40%的企业应用嵌入某种形式的AI代理（AI Agent）能力。这一预测不仅是一个数字，更反映了企业软件范式的根本性转变——从工具到伙伴，从被动响应到主动行动。

企业AI代理的嵌入方式正在从「外挂式」向「原生式」转变。早期的企业AI应用通常是在现有软件之上添加一个AI助手层——如在CRM系统中加入智能搜索或在ERP系统中加入预测分析。而2026年的趋势是将AI代理能力直接嵌入到软件的核心架构中，使AI成为业务流程的内在组成部分而非外在的附加功能。

在客户关系管理（CRM）领域，AI代理能够自动分析客户行为模式、预测客户需求、生成个性化的沟通方案，并在适当的时机自动执行跟进操作。Salesforce的Agentforce和Microsoft的Copilot for Sales代表了这一方向的最新进展。

在企业资源规划（ERP）领域，AI代理正在承担越来越复杂的供应链管理、财务分析和人力资源管理任务。它们能够实时监控业务指标、检测异常模式、提出优化建议，并在授权范围内自主执行调整操作。

安全性和可控性是企业AI代理部署的首要考虑。企业需要建立清晰的权限边界——代理可以自主执行哪些操作，哪些操作需要人工审批。审计追踪和可解释性也是企业级部署的基本要求。2026年的企业AI代理框架普遍提供了细粒度的权限控制和完整的操作日志，使企业能够在享受AI自动化效率的同时保持对业务流程的掌控力。

articles.title: 量子计算2025-2026：从实验室走向现实世界
articles.subtitle: 量子计算最新研究与技术进展
articles.author: SpinQuanta
articles.summary: 量子计算最新突破：Google Willow芯片实现指数级纠错，IBM发布Starling路线图，Microsoft推出Majorana 1拓扑量子比特，全球量子战略投资竞赛加速。
articles.content_en:
Quantum Computing News: ICQE 2025 & Latest Quantum Research

 2025.06.17 · Blog quantum computing news quantum research 

 
 

 Quantum computing is rapidly advancing on multiple fronts, from hardware innovations to real-world demonstrations and government strategies. In June 2025, the International Conference on Quantum Energy (ICQE 2025) in Padua, Italy, drew global attention to the need for quantum research to align with energy and sustainability goals. 

 In this article, we cover key takeaways from ICQE 2025 and a broader roundup of recent quantum computing news and quantum research. 

 
 
 
 ICQE 2025 – Energy & Responsibility: The conference emphasized that "the cost of energy will define the cost of intelligence," urging quantum development with sustainability in mind. Researchers discussed using quantum tools for renewable energy challenges and making computing more efficient. 
 
 
 

 
 
 Major Tech Breakthroughs: Google's 105-qubit Willow chip demonstrated exponential error reduction and ran a benchmark in ~5 minutes that would take a classical supercomputer ~10^25 years. IBM unveiled its fault-tolerant roadmap Starling , a 2029 target system with 200 logical qubits (100 million operations). Microsoft introduced Majorana 1 , a new topological qubit architecture aiming to scale to millions of qubits. 
 
 
 

 
 
 Quantum Advantage in Action: In March 2025, IonQ and Ansys ran a medical-device simulation on IonQ's 36-qubit computer, achieving a ~12% speed-up over classical HPC. This is one of the first cases showing a practical quantum outperforming a classical method. Researchers also used quantum simulation to solve a 40-year puzzle about quasicrystals: University of Michigan scientists proved these exotic materials are fundamentally stable by simulating their atomic structure with cutting-edge quantum algorithms. 
 
 
 

 
 
 Hardware Milestones: In April 2025, Fujitsu and RIKEN announced a 256-qubit superconducting quantum computer – four times larger than their 2023 – and set plans for a 1,000-qubit machine by 2026. These scale-ups enable tackling more complex problems (larger molecules, advanced error correction) on hybrid quantum-classical platforms. 
 
 
 

 

 ICQE 2025: Energy Meets Quantum Responsibility 

 
 

 ICQE 2025 drove home that as quantum and AI scale up, their energy demands cannot be overlooked. Co-chair Francesco Campaioli framed it starkly: "It is the cost of energy that ultimately will define the cost of intelligence". Talks stressed that quantum technology should be built from the ground up for efficiency and sustainability, not retrofitted later. 

 For example, speakers highlighted three key areas: foundational physics, efficient computing, and materials discovery. Understanding quantum thermodynamics could lead to new low-energy cooling and power distribution strategies. 

 Architectures like biased cat-qubit error correction were shown to reduce the energy overhead of logical qubits. And quantum simulation/sensing offers faster ways to design next-generation energy materials – from solar cells to hydrogen catalysts – by modeling their quantum behavior directly. 

 ICQE also underscored the need for collaboration across fields. Scientists, engineers, industry and policymakers were urged to break out of silos and share a unified vision. On the political side, Italy used the event to announce an upcoming National Quantum Strategy. Physicist Simone Montangero revealed that Italy will create an "Italian Quantum Alliance" uniting universities, research institutes and companies under this strategy. This move – integrating quantum into broader technology and energy policies – reflects a trend. As one insider put it, "signals like this influence how partnerships are formed and how seriously the public and private sectors take the work ahead". 

 In short, ICQE 2025 highlighted that quantum's future is not just faster computing, but smarter, sustainable growth aligned with real-world needs. 

 

 

 

 Latest Breakthroughs in Quantum Hardware and Algorithms 

 Top quantum computing companies reported major quantum leaps. In December 2024, Google announced Willow , a 105-qubit superconducting processor, as its new flagship. Willow's standout achievement is exponential error correction: the more physical qubits used, the lower the overall error rate – a milestone known as going "below threshold". 

 Google also ran the standard random-circuit sampling benchmark: Willow solved it in under 5 minutes, a task that the fastest classical supercomputer would take ~10^25 years to match. These results, described in Nature , are strong evidence that large, error-corrected quantum computers can be built in the future. 

 

 

 Also in 2025, IBM unveiled a roadmap for fault-tolerant quantum computing. The centerpiece is IBM Quantum Starling , targeted for 2029: a large-scale quantum computer with 200 logical qubits (running ~100 million error-co
articles.content_cn:
量子计算在2025至2026年间取得了多项里程碑式的进展，正在加速从实验室研究走向现实世界的应用部署。虽然通用量子计算机的实现仍然面临重大挑战，但在特定领域的量子优势已经得到了令人信服的验证。

IBM在2025年推出的Condor处理器拥有超过1000个量子比特，标志着量子硬件规模的持续扩展。更重要的是，IBM在量子纠错技术上的进展使得逻辑量子比特的错误率首次降低到了实用化门槛以下。这意味着量子计算的可靠性正在从「实验室可接受」提升到「工业级可靠」的水平。

Google的量子AI团队在量子化学模拟方面取得了突破性进展。利用其最新的量子处理器，Google的研究人员成功地模拟了此前无法用经典计算机精确处理的化学反应过程。这一成果对药物发现和新材料设计具有直接的商业价值。

中国的量子计算生态也在快速成长。本源量子和国盾量子等公司正在构建从量子芯片到量子软件的完整技术栈。「祖冲之号」量子计算机在特定计算任务上的表现已经达到了国际领先水平。量子通信领域，中国的「墨子号」量子卫星实现了千公里级的量子密钥分发，为量子安全通信网络的建设奠定了基础。

量子计算的商业化路径正在变得更加清晰。量子-经典混合计算模式作为通向完全量子计算的中期方案，已经在化学模拟、金融风险分析和组合优化等领域展现了实际价值。越来越多的企业开始设立量子计算战略团队，评估量子技术对其业务的潜在影响，并为量子优势的到来做好准备。
